fullCardInfo.name: Exeggcute
fullCardInfo.description: Though it may look like it's just a bunch of eggs, it's a proper Pokémon. Exeggcute communicates with others of its kind via telepathy, apparently.

fullCardInfo.name: Masquerain
fullCardInfo.description: It flaps its four wings to hover and fly freely in any direction—to and fro and sideways.

fullCardInfo.name: Maractus
fullCardInfo.description: With noises that could be mistaken for the rattles of maracas, it creates an upbeat rhythm, startling bird Pokémon and making them fly off in a hurry.

fullCardInfo.name: Karrablast
fullCardInfo.description: Its strange physiology reacts to electrical energy in interesting ways. The presence of a Shelmet will cause this Pokémon to evolve.

fullCardInfo.name: Rowlet
fullCardInfo.description: It sends its feathers, which are as sharp as blades, flying in attack. Its legs are strong, so its kicks are also formidable.

attacks.name: Skill Dive
attacks.effect: This attack does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Dartrix
fullCardInfo.description: This narcissistic Pokémon is a clean freak. If you don't groom it diligently, it may stop listening to you.

fullCardInfo.name: Grubbin
fullCardInfo.description: Its natural enemies, like Rookidee, may flee rather than risk getting caught in its large mandibles that can snap thick tree branches.

fullCardInfo.name: Fomantis
fullCardInfo.description: Fomantis hates having its naps interrupted. It fires off beams using energy it gathers by bathing in the sun.

fullCardInfo.name: Lurantis
fullCardInfo.description: By masquerading as a bug Pokémon, it lowers the guard of actual bug Pokémon lured in by a scent of sweet flowers. Its sickles bring them down.

attacks.name: Petal Blizzard
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Morelull
fullCardInfo.description: Pokémon living in the forest eat the delicious caps on Morelull's head. The caps regrow overnight.

fullCardInfo.name: Shiinotic
fullCardInfo.description: Its flickering spores lure in prey and put them to sleep. Once this Pokémon has its prey snoozing, it drains their vitality with its fingertips.

attacks.name: Flickering Light
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon can't attack during your opponent's next turn.

fullCardInfo.name: Growlithe
fullCardInfo.description: It has a brave and trustworthy nature. It fearlessly stands up to bigger and stronger foes.

fullCardInfo.name: Fletchinder
fullCardInfo.description: Fletchinder scatters embers in tall grass where bug Pokémon might be hiding and then catches them as they come leaping out.

fullCardInfo.name: Litten
fullCardInfo.description: If you try too hard to get close to it, it won't open up to you. Even if you do grow close, giving it too much affection is still a no-no.

attacks.name: Heat Tackle
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

fullCardInfo.name: Torracat
fullCardInfo.description: Torracat will let its Trainer coddle it once they've grown close, but it's a powerful, sharp-clawed Pokémon, so its Trainer gets covered in scratches.

attacks.name: Heat Tackle
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

fullCardInfo.name: Alolan Sandshrew
fullCardInfo.description: Life on mountains covered with deep snow has granted this Pokémon a body of ice that's as hard as steel.

fullCardInfo.name: Alolan Sandslash
fullCardInfo.description: It uses large, hooked claws to cut a path through deep snow as it runs. On snowy mountains, this Sandslash is faster than any other Pokémon.

attacks.name: Spike Armor
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, if this Pokémon is damaged by an attack, do 40 damage to the Attacking Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Alolan Vulpix
fullCardInfo.description: After long years in the ever-snowcapped mountains of Alola, this Vulpix has gained power over ice.

attacks.name: Call Forth Cold
attacks.effect: Take a {W} Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to this Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Lapras
fullCardInfo.description: A smart and kindhearted Pokémon, it glides across the surface of the sea while its beautiful song echoes around it.

fullCardInfo.name: Popplio
fullCardInfo.description: The balloons it inflates with its nose grow larger and larger as it practices day by day.

attacks.name: Sing
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

fullCardInfo.name: Brionne
fullCardInfo.description: It gets excited when it sees a dance it doesn't know. This hard worker practices diligently until it can learn that dance.

fullCardInfo.name: Dewpider
fullCardInfo.description: It forms a water bubble at the rear of its body and then covers its head with it. Meeting another Dewpider means comparing water-bubble sizes.

fullCardInfo.name: Araquanid
fullCardInfo.description: It launches water bubbles with its legs, drowning prey within the bubbles. This Pokémon can then take its time to savor its meal.

attacks.name: Dangerous Claws
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Basic Pokémon, this attack does 60 more damage.

fullCardInfo.name: Pyukumuku
fullCardInfo.description: It lives in warm, shallow waters. If it encounters a foe, it will spit out its internal organs as a means to punch them.

abilities.name: Innards Out
abilities.effect: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, do 50 damage to the Attacking Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Bruxish
fullCardInfo.description: It grinds its teeth with great force to stimulate its brain. It fires the psychic energy created by this process from the protuberance on its head.

fullCardInfo.name: Pikachu
fullCardInfo.description: When it is angered, it immediately discharges the energy stored in the pouches in its cheeks.

fullCardInfo.name: Alolan Golem
fullCardInfo.description: It's grumpy and stubborn. If you upset it, it discharges electricity from the surface of its body and growls with a voice like thunder.

attacks.name: Super Zap Cannon
attacks.effect: Discard 2 {L} Energy from this Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Helioptile
fullCardInfo.description: When spread, the frills on its head act like solar panels, generating the power behind this Pokémon's electric moves.

fullCardInfo.name: Charjabug
fullCardInfo.description: While its durable shell protects it from attacks, Charjabug strikes at enemies with jolts of electricity discharged from the tips of its jaws.

fullCardInfo.name: Sableye
fullCardInfo.description: It dwells in the darkness of caves. It uses its sharp claws to dig up gems to nourish itself.

attacks.name: Corner
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

fullCardInfo.name: Spoink
fullCardInfo.description: Spoink will die if it stops bouncing. The pearl on its head amplifies its psychic powers.

attacks.name: Psycharge
attacks.effect: Take a {P} Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to this Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Grumpig
fullCardInfo.description: It can perform odd dance steps to influence foes. Its style of dancing became hugely popular overseas.

fullCardInfo.name: Lunatone
fullCardInfo.description: The phase of the moon apparently has some effect on its power. It's active on the night of a full moon.

fullCardInfo.name: Shuppet
fullCardInfo.description: It loves to feed on feelings like envy and malice. Its upright horn catches the emotions of people.

fullCardInfo.name: Banette
fullCardInfo.description: This Pokémon developed from an abandoned doll that amassed a grudge. It is seen in dark alleys.

attacks.name: Night Bind
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, they can't take any Energy from their Energy Zone to attach to their Active Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Oricorio
fullCardInfo.description: This form of Oricorio has sipped pink nectar. It elevates its mind with the gentle steps of its dance, then unleashes its psychic energy.

attacks.name: Dazzle Dance
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

fullCardInfo.name: Oricorio
fullCardInfo.description: This form of Oricorio has sipped purple nectar. It uses ethereal dance steps to call forth the spirits of the dead.

attacks.name: Spiteful Dance
attacks.effect: If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an attack during your opponent's last turn, this attack does 60 more damage.

fullCardInfo.name: Ribombee
fullCardInfo.description: It makes pollen puffs from pollen and nectar. The puffs' effects depend on the type of ingredients and how much of each one is used.

fullCardInfo.name: Sandygast
fullCardInfo.description: If it loses its shovel, it will stick something else—like a branch—in its head to make do until it finds another shovel.

fullCardInfo.name: Cosmog
fullCardInfo.description: Even though its helpless, gaseous body can be blown away by the slightest breeze, it doesn't seem to care.

attacks.name: Teleport
attacks.effect: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Cosmoem
fullCardInfo.description:
The king who ruled Alola in times of antiquity called it
the “cocoon of the stars” and built an altar to worship it.

attacks.name: Stiffen
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes −50 damage from attacks.

fullCardInfo.name: Necrozma
fullCardInfo.description: It looks somehow pained as it rages around in search of light, which serves as its energy. It's apparently from another world.

attacks.name: Prismatic Laser
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

fullCardInfo.name: Cubone
fullCardInfo.description: When the memory of its departed mother brings it to tears, its cries echo mournfully within the skull it wears on its head.

fullCardInfo.name: Hariyama
fullCardInfo.description: It loves challenging others to tests of strength. It has the power to stop a train with a slap.

attacks.name: Push Out
attacks.effect: Switch out your opponent's Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)

fullCardInfo.name: Drilbur
fullCardInfo.description: It brings its claws together and whirls around at high speed before rushing toward its prey.

fullCardInfo.name: Timburr
fullCardInfo.description: It loves helping out with construction projects. It loves it so much that if rain causes work to halt, it swings its log around and throws a tantrum.

fullCardInfo.name: Gurdurr
fullCardInfo.description: It shows off its muscles to Machoke and other Gurdurr. If it fails to measure up to the other Pokémon, it lies low for a little while.

fullCardInfo.name: Conkeldurr
fullCardInfo.description: Concrete mixed by Conkeldurr is much more durable than normal concrete, even when the compositions of the two materials are the same.

abilities.name: Guts
abilities.effect: If this Pokémon would be Knocked Out by damage from an attack, flip a coin. If heads, this Pokémon is not Knocked Out, and its remaining HP becomes 10.

fullCardInfo.name: Crabrawler
fullCardInfo.description: This Pokémon punches trees and eats the berries that drop down, training itself and getting food at the same time.

fullCardInfo.name: Rockruff
fullCardInfo.description: This Pokémon is very friendly when it's young. Its disposition becomes vicious once it matures, but it never forgets the kindness of its master.

attacks.name: Signs of Evolution
attacks.effect: Put a random card that evolves from Rockruff from your deck into your hand.

fullCardInfo.name: Lycanroc
fullCardInfo.description: This Pokémon uses its rocky mane to slash any who approach. It will even disobey its Trainer if it dislikes the orders it was given.

attacks.name: Blood Fang
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon has more remaining HP than this Pokémon, this attack does 50 more damage.

fullCardInfo.name: Mudsdale
fullCardInfo.description: This Pokémon has been treasured not just for its physical labor but also because it produces high-quality mud used for making pottery.

attacks.name: High Horsepower
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 40 damage to itself.

abilities.name: Offload Pass
abilities.effect: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, move all {F} Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Minior
fullCardInfo.description: Places where Minior fall from the night sky are few and far between, with Alola being one of the precious few.

attacks.name: Star Drop
attacks.effect: This attack does 40 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Alolan Rattata
fullCardInfo.description: It shows no interest in anything that isn't fresh. If you take it shopping with you, it will help you pick out ingredients.

fullCardInfo.name: Alolan Meowth
fullCardInfo.description: It's accustomed to luxury because it used to live with Alolan royalty. As a result, it's very picky about food.

fullCardInfo.name: Alolan Grimer
fullCardInfo.description: There are a hundred or so of them living in Alola's waste-disposal site. They're all hard workers who eat a lot of trash.

fullCardInfo.name: Absol
fullCardInfo.description: Swift as the wind, Absol races through fields and mountains. Its curved, bow-like horn is acutely sensitive to the warning signs of natural disasters.

attacks.name: Unseen Claw
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is affected by a Special Condition, this attack does 60 more damage.

fullCardInfo.name: Trubbish
fullCardInfo.description: Its favorite places are unsanitary ones. If you leave trash lying around, you could even find one of these Pokémon living in your room.

fullCardInfo.name: Garbodor
fullCardInfo.description: This Pokémon eats trash, which turns into poison inside its body. The main component of the poison depends on what sort of trash was eaten.

attacks.name: Super Poison Breath
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

fullCardInfo.name: Mareanie
fullCardInfo.description: This Pokémon wanders the seaside looking for food. It often gets electric shocks from broken Pincurchin spines that it tries to eat.

fullCardInfo.name: Toxapex
fullCardInfo.description: This Pokémon lives within a dome made by its own legs. Toxapex monitors its surroundings by sensing the flow of the tide through its spikes.

attacks.name: Spike Cannon
attacks.effect: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.

fullCardInfo.name: Alolan Diglett
fullCardInfo.description: The metal-rich geology of this Pokémon's habitat caused it to develop steel whiskers on its head.

fullCardInfo.name: Alolan Dugtrio
fullCardInfo.description: Their beautiful, metallic whiskers create a sort of protective helmet on their heads, and they also function as highly precise sensors.

attacks.name: Iron Head
attacks.effect: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 70 damage for each heads.

fullCardInfo.name: Escavalier
fullCardInfo.description: Wearing the shell covering they stole from Shelmet, they defend themselves and attack with two lances.

attacks.name: Pike
attacks.effect: This attack also does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Klefki
fullCardInfo.description: Once it absorbs a key's metal ions, it discards the key without a second thought. However, it will hang on to keys it favors for decades.

attacks.name: Unlock
attacks.effect: Draw a card.

abilities.name: Rising Road
abilities.effect: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is on your Bench, you may switch it with your Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Sol Breaker
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

fullCardInfo.name: Drampa
fullCardInfo.description: The mountains it calls home are nearly two miles in height. On rare occasions, it descends to play with the children living in the towns below.

attacks.name: Berserk
attacks.effect: If any of your Benched Pokémon have damage on them, this attack does 50 more damage.

fullCardInfo.name: Hakamo-o
fullCardInfo.description: The scaleless, scarred parts of its body are signs of its strength. It shows them off to defeated opponents.

fullCardInfo.name: Tauros
fullCardInfo.description: When Tauros begins whipping itself with its tails, it's a warning that the Pokémon is about to charge with astounding speed.

attacks.name: Rising Lunge
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 50 more damage.

fullCardInfo.name: Skitty
fullCardInfo.description: It can't stop itself from chasing moving things, and it runs in a circle, chasing its own tail.

fullCardInfo.name: Delcatty
fullCardInfo.description: It dislikes dirty places. It often searches for a comfortable place in which to groom itself.

attacks.name: Energy Assist
attacks.effect: Take a {C} Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Pikipek
fullCardInfo.description: It pecks at trees with its hard beak. You can get some idea of its mood or condition from the rhythm of its pecking.

fullCardInfo.name: Trumbeak
fullCardInfo.description: It can bend the tip of its beak to produce over a hundred different cries at will.

fullCardInfo.name: Yungoos
fullCardInfo.description: Its stomach fills most of its torso. It wanders the same path every day, searching for fresh food.

fullCardInfo.name: Gumshoos
fullCardInfo.description: Once it finds signs of prey, it will patiently stake out the location, waiting until the sun goes down.

fullCardInfo.name: Stufful
fullCardInfo.description: Its fluffy fur is a delight to pet, but carelessly reaching out to touch this Pokémon could result in painful retaliation.

fullCardInfo.name: Komala
fullCardInfo.description: Komala spends its entire life sleeping. It feeds on leaves that contain a potent poison only Komala can break down.

abilities.name: Comatose
abilities.effect: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, whenever you attach an Energy from your Energy Zone to it, it is now Asleep.

fullCardInfo.name: Big Malasada
fullCardInfo.effect: Heal 10 damage and remove a random Special Condition from your Active Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Rare Candy
fullCardInfo.effect: Choose 1 of your Basic Pokémon in play. If you have a Stage 2 card in your hand that evolves from that Pokémon, put that card onto the Basic Pokémon to evolve it, skipping the Stage 1. You can't use this card during your first turn or on a Basic Pokémon that was put into play this turn.

fullCardInfo.name: Leaf Cape
fullCardInfo.effect: The {G} Pokémon this card is attached to gets +30 HP.

fullCardInfo.name: Acerola
fullCardInfo.effect: Choose 1 of your Palossand or Mimikyu that has damage on it, and move 40 of its damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Kiawe
fullCardInfo.effect: Choose 1 of your Alolan Marowak or Turtonator. Take 2 {R} Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to that Pokémon. Your turn ends.

fullCardInfo.name: Guzma
fullCardInfo.effect: Discard all Pokémon Tool cards attached to each of your opponent's Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Mallow
fullCardInfo.effect: Heal all damage from 1 of your Shiinotic or Tsareena. If you do, discard all Energy from that Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Lillie
fullCardInfo.effect: Heal 60 damage from 1 of your Stage 2 Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Alolan Marowak
fullCardInfo.description: This Pokémon sets the bone it holds on fire and dances through the night as a way to mourn its fallen allies.

attacks.name: Burning Bonemerang
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 70 damage for each heads. If at least 1 of them is heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.name: Bonemerang
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 80 damage for each heads.

fullCardInfo.name: Bulbasaur
fullCardInfo.description:
While it is young, it uses the nutrients that are
stored in the seed on its back in order to grow.

fullCardInfo.name: Ivysaur
fullCardInfo.description:
The more sunlight Ivysaur bathes in, the more
strength wells up within it, allowing the bud
on its back to grow larger.

attacks.name: Synthesis
attacks.effect: Take 2 {G} Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to this Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Spinarak
fullCardInfo.description:
Although the poison from its fangs isn't that
strong, it's potent enough to weaken prey that
gets caught in its web.

fullCardInfo.name: Ariados
fullCardInfo.description:
It spews threads from its mouth to catch its
prey. When night falls, it leaves its web to go
hunt aggressively.

abilities.name: Trap Territory
abilities.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon's Retreat Cost is 1 more.

fullCardInfo.name: Sunkern
fullCardInfo.description:
It is very weak. Its only means of defense is to
shake its leaves desperately at its attacker.

fullCardInfo.name: Sunflora
fullCardInfo.description:
Since it converts sunlight into energy, it is always
looking in the direction of the sun.

attacks.name: Quick-Grow Beam
attacks.effect: If Quick-Grow Extract is in your discard pile, this attack does 30 more damage.

fullCardInfo.name: Burmy
fullCardInfo.description:
If its cloak is broken in battle, it quickly remakes
the cloak with materials nearby.

fullCardInfo.name: Mothim
fullCardInfo.description:
It flutters around at night and steals honey from
the Combee hive.

fullCardInfo.name: Charmander
fullCardInfo.description:
From the time it is born, a flame burns at the tip
of its tail. Its life would end if the flame were to
go out.

fullCardInfo.name: Charmeleon
fullCardInfo.description:
If it becomes agitated during battle, it spouts
intense flames, incinerating its surroundings.

abilities.name: Ignition
abilities.effect: Once during your turn, when you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may take a {R} Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to your Active {R} Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Charizard
fullCardInfo.description:
Its wings can carry this Pokémon close to an
altitude of 4,600 feet. It blows out fire at very
high temperatures.

attacks.name: Inferno Onrush
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

fullCardInfo.name: Mega Charizard Y ex
fullCardInfo.description:
Its bond with its Trainer is the source of
its power. It boasts speed and maneuverability
greater than that of a jet.

attacks.name: Crimson Dive
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 50 damage to itself.

fullCardInfo.name: Houndour
fullCardInfo.description:
It uses different kinds of cries for communicating
with others of its kind and for pursuing its prey.

attacks.name: Roar
attacks.effect: Switch out your opponent's Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)

fullCardInfo.name: Houndoom
fullCardInfo.description:
Upon hearing its eerie howls, other Pokémon get
the shivers and head straight back to their nests.

fullCardInfo.name: Squirtle
fullCardInfo.description:
When it feels threatened, it draws its limbs inside
its shell and sprays water from its mouth.

fullCardInfo.name: Wartortle
fullCardInfo.description:
It cleverly controls its furry ears and tail to
maintain its balance while swimming.

abilities.name: Shell Shield
abilities.effect: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks.

fullCardInfo.name: Mega Blastoise ex
fullCardInfo.description:
The cannon on its back is as powerful as a
tank gun. Its tough legs and back enable it to
withstand the recoil from firing the cannon.

attacks.name: Triple Bombardment
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has at least 3 extra {W} Energy attached, this attack also does 50 damage to 2 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Basculin
fullCardInfo.description:
Anglers love the fight this Pokémon puts up on
the hook. And there are always more to catch—
many people release them into lakes illicitly.

attacks.name: Surprise Strike
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon moved from your Bench to the Active Spot this turn, this attack does 40 more damage.

fullCardInfo.name: Clawitzer
fullCardInfo.description:
The cannonballs of seawater that Clawitzer
launches from its claw are powerful enough to
punch through tanker hulls.

attacks.name: Aqua Shower
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Magnemite
fullCardInfo.description:
The electromagnetic waves emitted by the units
at the sides of its head expel antigravity, which
allows it to float.

fullCardInfo.name: Emolga
fullCardInfo.description:
This Pokémon absolutely loves sweet berries.
Sometimes it stuffs its cheeks full of so much
food that it can't fly properly.

fullCardInfo.name: Helioptile
fullCardInfo.description:
The sun powers this Pokémon's electricity
generation. Interruption of that process
stresses Helioptile to the point of weakness.

attacks.name: Thunder Shock
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

fullCardInfo.name: Misdreavus
fullCardInfo.description:
It loves to bite and yank people's hair from
behind without warning, just to see their
shocked reactions.

attacks.name: Lead
attacks.effect: Put a random Supporter card from your deck into your hand.

fullCardInfo.name: Solosis
fullCardInfo.description:
Many say that the special liquid covering this
Pokémon's body would allow it to survive in
the vacuum of space.

fullCardInfo.name: Duosion
fullCardInfo.description:
Its brain has split into two, and the two
halves rarely think alike. Its actions are
utterly unpredictable.

fullCardInfo.name: Reuniclus
fullCardInfo.description:
It's said that drinking the liquid surrounding
Reuniclus grants wisdom. Problem is, the liquid is
highly toxic to anything besides Reuniclus itself.

abilities.name: Infinite Increase
abilities.effect: This Pokémon gets +30 HP for each {P} Energy attached to it.

fullCardInfo.name: Spritzee
fullCardInfo.description:
The scent its body gives off enraptures those
who smell it. Noble ladies had no shortage of
love for Spritzee.

attacks.name: Sweet Scent
attacks.effect: Heal 20 damage from 1 of your Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Aromatisse
fullCardInfo.description:
The scents Aromatisse can produce range from
sweet smells that bolster allies to foul smells
that sap an opponent's will to fight.

attacks.name: Perfume Blast
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, attacks used by the Defending Pokémon cost 1 {C} more.

fullCardInfo.name: Xerneas
fullCardInfo.description:
When the horns on its head shine
in seven colors, it is said to be
sharing everlasting life.

attacks.name: Geoburst
attacks.effect: This attack's damage is reduced by the amount of damage this Pokémon has on it.

fullCardInfo.name: Onix
fullCardInfo.description:
It rapidly bores through the ground at 50 mph by
squirming and twisting its massive, rugged body.

attacks.name: Dig
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from—and effects of—attacks done to this Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Makuhita
fullCardInfo.description:
It toughens up by slamming into thick trees
over and over. It gains a sturdy body and
dauntless spirit.

fullCardInfo.name: Hariyama
fullCardInfo.description:
It stomps on the ground to build power.
It can send a 10-ton truck flying with a
straight-arm punch.

fullCardInfo.name: Mienfoo
fullCardInfo.description:
Though small, Mienfoo's temperament is fierce.
Any creature that approaches Mienfoo carelessly
will be greeted with a flurry of graceful attacks.

fullCardInfo.name: Grimer
fullCardInfo.description:
Appears in filthy areas. It thrives by sucking up
polluted sludge that is pumped out of factories.

fullCardInfo.name: Muk
fullCardInfo.description:
They love to gather in smelly areas where sludge
accumulates, making the stench around
them worse.

attacks.name: Gunk Shot
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

fullCardInfo.name: Purrloin
fullCardInfo.description:
Opponents that get drawn in by its adorable
behavior come away with stinging scratches from
its claws and stinging pride from its laughter.

attacks.name: Trip Over
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

fullCardInfo.name: Trubbish
fullCardInfo.description:
Its favorite places are unsanitary ones. If you
leave trash lying around, you could even find
one of these Pokémon living in your room.

fullCardInfo.name: Steelix
fullCardInfo.description:
It is thought its body transformed as a result of
iron accumulating internally from swallowing soil.

attacks.name: Metal Defender
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon has no Weakness.

fullCardInfo.name: Probopass
fullCardInfo.description:
It exudes strong magnetism from all over.
It controls three small units called Mini-Noses.

attacks.name: Metallic Turbo
attacks.effect: Take 2 {M} Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Porygon
fullCardInfo.description:
State-of-the-art technology was used to create
Porygon. It was the first artificial Pokémon to be
created via computer programming.

attacks.name: Stiffen
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes −20 damage from attacks.

fullCardInfo.name: Porygon-Z
fullCardInfo.description:
Porygon-Z had a program installed to allow it to
move between dimensions, but the program also
caused instability in Porygon-Z's behavior.

attacks.name: Cyberjack
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 more damage for each Trainer card in your opponent's deck.

fullCardInfo.name: Starly
fullCardInfo.description:
Because they are weak individually, they form
groups. However, they bicker if the group grows
too big.

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.effect: Put 1 random Starly from your deck onto your Bench.

fullCardInfo.name: Staravia
fullCardInfo.description:
They maintain huge flocks, although fierce
scuffles break out between various flocks.

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

fullCardInfo.name: Staraptor
fullCardInfo.description:
It never stops attacking even if it is injured.
It fusses over the shape of its comb.

attacks.name: Hurricane Wing
attacks.effect: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 50 damage for each heads.

fullCardInfo.name: Buneary
fullCardInfo.description:
Buneary can attack by rolling up their ears and
then striking with the force created by unrolling
them. This attack becomes stronger with training.

attacks.name: Double Kick
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.

fullCardInfo.name: Lopunny
fullCardInfo.description:
Once hot seasons are over, Lopunny's coat will
be replaced with fur that holds a lot of insulating
air in preparation for colder weather.

attacks.name: Double Kick
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 50 damage for each heads.

fullCardInfo.name: Bouffalant
fullCardInfo.description:
These Pokémon live in herds of about 20
individuals. Bouffalant that betray the herd will
lose the hair on their heads for some reason.

attacks.name: Revenge
attacks.effect: If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an attack during your opponent's last turn, this attack does 40 more damage.

fullCardInfo.name: Furfrou
fullCardInfo.description:
Left alone, its fur will grow longer and longer,
but it will only allow someone it trusts to cut it.

abilities.name: Fur Coat
abilities.effect: This Pokémon takes −20 damage from attacks.

fullCardInfo.name: Clemont
fullCardInfo.effect: Put 2 random cards from among Magneton, Heliolisk, and Clemont's Backpack from your deck into your hand.

fullCardInfo.name: Serena
fullCardInfo.effect: Put a random Mega Evolution Pokémon ex from your deck into your hand.

fullCardInfo.name: Ariados
fullCardInfo.description:
It spews threads from its mouth to catch its
prey. When night falls, it leaves its web to go
hunt aggressively.

abilities.name: Trap Territory
abilities.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon's Retreat Cost is 1 more.

fullCardInfo.name: Reuniclus
fullCardInfo.description:
It's said that drinking the liquid surrounding
Reuniclus grants wisdom. Problem is, the liquid is
highly toxic to anything besides Reuniclus itself.

abilities.name: Infinite Increase
abilities.effect: This Pokémon gets +30 HP for each {P} Energy attached to it.

fullCardInfo.name: Bounsweet
fullCardInfo.description:
Its sweat is sweet, like syrup made from boiled-down
fruit. Because of this, Bounsweet was highly valued in
the past, when sweeteners were scarce.

fullCardInfo.name: Tsareena
fullCardInfo.description:
This Pokémon is proud and aggressive. However, it is
said that a Tsareena will instantly become calm if
someone touches the crown on its calyx.

attacks.name: Kick Down
attacks.effect: Your opponent reveals a random card from their hand and shuffles it into their deck.

fullCardInfo.name: Applin
fullCardInfo.description:
It grows by eating the flesh of an apple.
Applin uses its own body fluid to strengthen
the apple's skin and prevent it from rotting.

fullCardInfo.name: Appletun
fullCardInfo.description:
It feeds mainly on sweet apples. It will also eat
small bug Pokémon that are attracted by its
sweet nectar.

attacks.name: Sweets Relay
attacks.effect: If 1 of your Pokémon used Sweets Relay during your last turn, this attack does 30 more damage.

fullCardInfo.name: Flareon
fullCardInfo.description:
Inhaled air is carried to its flame sac, heated, and
exhaled as fire that reaches over 3,000 degrees
Fahrenheit.

attacks.name: Assisting Heater
attacks.effect: Take a {R} Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

abilities.name: Combust
abilities.effect: Once during your turn, you may attach a {R} Energy from your discard pile to this Pokémon. If you do, do 20 damage to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Fire Spin
attacks.effect: Discard 2 {R} Energy from this Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Torkoal
fullCardInfo.description:
It burns coal inside its shell for energy. It blows
out black soot if it is endangered.

attacks.name: Flamethrower
attacks.effect: Discard a {R} Energy from this Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Litten
fullCardInfo.description:
If you try too hard to get close to it, it won't
open up to you. Even if you do grow close,
giving it too much affection is still a no-no.

fullCardInfo.name: Torracat
fullCardInfo.description:
Torracat will let its Trainer coddle it once they've
grown close, but it's a powerful, sharp-clawed
Pokémon, so its Trainer gets covered in scratches.

fullCardInfo.name: Incineroar
fullCardInfo.description:
Although it's rough mannered and egotistical, it
finds beating down unworthy opponents boring.
It gets motivated for stronger opponents.

attacks.name: Darkest Lariat
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 100 damage for each heads.

fullCardInfo.name: Vanillite
fullCardInfo.description:
Unable to survive in hot areas, it makes itself
comfortable by breathing out air cold enough to
cause snow. It burrows into the snow to sleep.

attacks.name: Sweets Relay
attacks.effect: If 1 of your Pokémon used Sweets Relay during your last turn, this attack does 20 more damage.

fullCardInfo.name: Vanilluxe
fullCardInfo.description:
When its anger reaches a breaking point, this
Pokémon unleashes a fierce blizzard that freezes
every creature around it, be they friend or foe.

attacks.name: Double Spin
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 80 damage for each heads.

fullCardInfo.name: Alomomola
fullCardInfo.description:
It gently holds injured and weak Pokémon in its
fins. Its special membrane heals their wounds.

attacks.name: Water Pulse
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

fullCardInfo.name: Popplio
fullCardInfo.description:
The balloons it inflates with its nose grow
larger and larger as it practices day by day.

attacks.name: Hydro Pump
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has at least 1 extra {W} Energy attached, this attack does 40 more damage.

attacks.name: Sparkling Aria
attacks.effect: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Joltik
fullCardInfo.description:
Joltik can be found clinging to other Pokémon.
It's soaking up static electricity because it can't
produce a charge on its own.

attacks.name: Jolt
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

fullCardInfo.name: Galvantula
fullCardInfo.description:
It launches electrified fur from its abdomen as its
means of attack. Opponents hit by the fur could
be in for three full days and nights of paralysis.

attacks.name: Electric Shock
attacks.effect: Discard all Energy attached to this Pokémon. Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

fullCardInfo.name: Woobat
fullCardInfo.description:
While inside a cave, if you look up and see lots
of heart-shaped marks lining the walls, it's
evidence that Woobat live there.

fullCardInfo.name: Swirlix
fullCardInfo.description:
It eats its own weight in sugar every day.
If it doesn't get enough sugar, it becomes
incredibly grumpy.

attacks.name: Sweets Relay
attacks.effect: If 1 of your Pokémon used Sweets Relay during your last turn, this attack does 20 more damage.

fullCardInfo.name: Sylveon
fullCardInfo.description:
This Pokémon uses its ribbonlike feelers to send
a soothing aura into its opponents, erasing
their hostility.

attacks.name: Evoharmony
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 more damage for each Evolution Pokémon on your Bench.

fullCardInfo.name: Mimikyu
fullCardInfo.description:
This Pokémon lives in dark places untouched by
sunlight. When it appears before humans, it hides
itself under a cloth that resembles a Pikachu.

attacks.name: Try to Imitate
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, choose 1 of your opponent's Active Pokémon's attacks and use it as this attack.

fullCardInfo.name: Milcery
fullCardInfo.description:
This Pokémon was born from sweet-smelling
particles in the air. Its body is made of cream.

attacks.name: Sweets Relay
attacks.effect: If 1 of your Pokémon used Sweets Relay during your last turn, this attack does 20 more damage.

fullCardInfo.name: Alcremie
fullCardInfo.description:
When it trusts a Trainer, it will treat them
to berries it's decorated with cream.

attacks.name: Sweets Overload
attacks.effect: This attack does 40 damage for each time your Pokémon used Sweets Relay during this game.

fullCardInfo.name: Mienshao
fullCardInfo.description:
When Mienshao comes across a truly challenging
opponent, it will lighten itself by biting off the
fur on its arms.

fullCardInfo.name: Purrloin
fullCardInfo.description:
Opponents that get drawn in by its adorable
behavior come away with stinging scratches from
its claws and stinging pride from its laughter.

attacks.name: Playful Knockdown
attacks.effect: Discard all Pokémon Tools from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Meltan
fullCardInfo.description:
It dissolves and eats metal. Circulating liquid
metal within its body is how it generates energy.

fullCardInfo.name: Dratini
fullCardInfo.description:
It sheds many layers of skin as it grows larger.
During this process, it is protected by a rapid
waterfall.

fullCardInfo.name: Drampa
fullCardInfo.description:
The mountains it calls home are nearly two miles
in height. On rare occasions, it descends to play
with the children living in the towns below.

attacks.name: Dragon Breath
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

fullCardInfo.name: Eevee
fullCardInfo.description:
Its ability to evolve into many forms allows it to
adapt smoothly and perfectly to any environment.

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.effect: Draw a card.

abilities.name: Veevee 'volve
abilities.effect: This Pokémon can evolve into any Pokémon that evolves from Eevee if you play it from your hand onto this Pokémon. (This Pokémon can't evolve during your first turn or the turn you play it.)

abilities.name: Full-Mouth Manner
abilities.effect: At the end of your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, heal 20 damage from it.

attacks.name: Flop-Down Punch
attacks.effect: This Pokémon is now Asleep.

fullCardInfo.name: Aipom
fullCardInfo.description:
As it did more and more with its tail, its hands
became clumsy. It makes its nest high in
the treetops.

attacks.name: Double Hit
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.

fullCardInfo.name: Audino
fullCardInfo.description:
This Pokémon has a kind heart. By touching with its feelers,
Audino can gauge other creatures' feelings and physical conditions.

attacks.name: Do the Wave
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 damage for each of your Benched Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Minccino
fullCardInfo.description:
The way it brushes away grime with its tail
can be helpful when cleaning. But its focus on
spotlessness can make cleaning more of a hassle.

fullCardInfo.name: Cinccino
fullCardInfo.description:
Its body secretes oil that this Pokémon spreads
over its nest as a coating to protect it from dust.
Cinccino won't tolerate even a speck of the stuff.

fullCardInfo.name: Eevee Bag
fullCardInfo.effect:
Choose 1:

During this turn, attacks used by your Pokémon that evolve from Eevee do +10 damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

Heal 20 damage from each of your Pokémon that evolves from Eevee.

fullCardInfo.name: Leftovers
fullCardInfo.effect: At the end of your turn, if the Pokémon this card is attached to is in the Active Spot, heal 10 damage from that Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Petilil
fullCardInfo.description:
The leaves on its head grow right back even if
they fall out. These bitter leaves refresh those
who eat them.

fullCardInfo.name: Lilligant
fullCardInfo.description:
The fragrance of the garland on its head has
a relaxing effect, but taking care of it is very
difficult.

fullCardInfo.name: Rowlet
fullCardInfo.description:
It sends its feathers, which are as sharp
as blades, flying in attack. Its legs are strong,
so its kicks are also formidable.

attacks.name: Fury Attack
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.

attacks.name: Big Beat
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Big Beat.

fullCardInfo.name: Pheromosa
fullCardInfo.description:
A life-form that lives in another world, its body
is thin and supple, but it also possesses
great power.

attacks.name: Jump Blues
attacks.effect: This attack also does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Blacephalon
fullCardInfo.description:
It slithers toward people. Then, without warning, it triggers the
explosion of its own head. It's apparently one kind of Ultra Beast.

attacks.name: Beat Punk
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 70 damage to itself.

fullCardInfo.name: Carvanha
fullCardInfo.description:
It won't attack while it's alone—not even if it
spots prey. Instead, it waits for other Carvanha to
join it, and then the Pokémon attack as a group.

fullCardInfo.name: Shinx
fullCardInfo.description:
The extension and contraction of its muscles
generates electricity. It glows when in trouble.

fullCardInfo.name: Luxio
fullCardInfo.description:
Strong electricity courses through the tips of
its sharp claws. A light scratch causes fainting
in foes.

fullCardInfo.name: Luxray
fullCardInfo.description:
It can see clearly through walls to track down its
prey and seek its lost young.

abilities.name: Intimidating Fang
abilities.effect: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, attacks used by your opponent's Active Pokémon do −20 damage.

fullCardInfo.name: Zebstrika
fullCardInfo.description:
When this ill-tempered Pokémon runs wild, it
shoots lightning from its mane in all directions.

fullCardInfo.name: Emolga
fullCardInfo.description:
As Emolga flutters through the air, it crackles
with electricity. This Pokémon is cute, but it can
cause a lot of trouble.

fullCardInfo.name: Zeraora
fullCardInfo.description:
It electrifies its claws and tears its opponents
apart with them. Even if they dodge its attack,
they'll be electrocuted by the flying sparks.

abilities.name: Thunderclap Flash
abilities.effect: At the end of your first turn, take a {L} Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to this Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Clefairy
fullCardInfo.description:
It is said that happiness will come to those who
see a gathering of Clefairy dancing under a
full moon.

fullCardInfo.name: Clefable
fullCardInfo.description:
A timid fairy Pokémon that is rarely seen, it will
run and hide the moment it senses people.

fullCardInfo.name: Phantump
fullCardInfo.description:
With a voice like a human child's, it cries out to
lure adults deep into the forest, getting them lost
among the trees.

fullCardInfo.name: Trevenant
fullCardInfo.description:
People fear it due to a belief that it devours any
who try to cut down trees in its forest, but to the
Pokémon it shares its woods with, it's kind.

fullCardInfo.name: Morelull
fullCardInfo.description:
It scatters spores that flicker and glow. Anyone
seeing these lights falls into a deep slumber.

fullCardInfo.name: Medicham
fullCardInfo.description:
Through yoga training, it gained the psychic
power to predict its foe's next move.

fullCardInfo.name: Baltoy
fullCardInfo.description:
It moves while spinning around on its single foot.
Some Baltoy have been seen spinning on
their heads.

fullCardInfo.name: Passimian
fullCardInfo.description:
This Pokémon battles by throwing hard berries.
It won't obey a Trainer who throws Poké Balls
without skill.

attacks.name: Let's Throw
attacks.effect: If Passimian is on your Bench, this attack does 40 more damage.

fullCardInfo.name: Sandygast
fullCardInfo.description:
If it loses its shovel, it will stick something
else—like a branch—in its head to make
do until it finds another shovel.

fullCardInfo.name: Alolan Persian
fullCardInfo.description:
The round face of Alolan Persian is considered
to be a symbol of prosperity in the Alola region,
so these Pokémon are very well cared for.

attacks.name: Fake Out
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

fullCardInfo.name: Krookodile
fullCardInfo.description:
After clamping down with its powerful jaws, it
twists its body around to rip its prey in half.

attacks.name: Poaching Fangs
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. For each heads, a card is chosen at random from your opponent's hand. Your opponent reveals that card and shuffles it into their deck.

fullCardInfo.name: Alolan Diglett
fullCardInfo.description:
The metal-rich geology of this Pokémon's habitat
caused it to develop steel whiskers on its head.

attacks.name: Happened to Headbutt
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

fullCardInfo.name: Aron
fullCardInfo.description:
It eats iron ore—and sometimes railroad tracks—
to build up the steel armor that protects its body.

fullCardInfo.name: Lairon
fullCardInfo.description:
It loves iron ore. Groups of them fight for territory
by bashing one another with their steel bodies.

fullCardInfo.name: Aggron
fullCardInfo.description:
Aggron has a horn sharp enough to perforate
thick iron sheets. It brings down its opponents by
ramming into them horn first.

attacks.name: Giga Impact
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

fullCardInfo.name: Herdier
fullCardInfo.description:
Herdier is a very smart and friendly Pokémon.
So much so that there's a theory that Herdier was
the first Pokémon to partner with people.

fullCardInfo.name: Stoutland
fullCardInfo.description:
These Pokémon seem to enjoy living with humans.
Even a Stoutland caught in the wild will warm up
to people in about three days.

abilities.name: Guard Dog Visage
abilities.effect: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, attacks used by your opponent's Active Pokémon cost 1 {C} more.

fullCardInfo.name: Stufful
fullCardInfo.description:
Its fluffy fur is a delight to pet, but carelessly reaching out
to touch this Pokémon could result in painful retaliation.

fullCardInfo.name: Bewear
fullCardInfo.description:
Once it accepts you as a friend, it tries to show
its affection with a hug. Letting it do that is
dangerous—it could easily shatter your bones.

attacks.name: Superpowered Hug
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. If both of them are heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is Knocked Out.

fullCardInfo.name: Type: Null
fullCardInfo.description:
A Pokémon weapon developed for a specific
mission, it went berserk during an experiment,
so it was cryogenically frozen.

attacks.name: Quick Blow
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

fullCardInfo.name: Beastite
fullCardInfo.effect: Attacks used by the Ultra Beast this card is attached to do +10 damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon for each point you have gotten.

fullCardInfo.name: Looker
fullCardInfo.effect: Your opponent reveals all of the Supporter cards in their deck.

fullCardInfo.name: Rowlet
fullCardInfo.description:
It sends its feathers, which are as sharp
as blades, flying in attack. Its legs are strong,
so its kicks are also formidable.

attacks.name: Fury Attack
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.

fullCardInfo.name: Pidgey
fullCardInfo.description:
A common sight in forests and woods. It flaps
its wings at ground level to kick up blinding sand.

fullCardInfo.name: Ledyba
fullCardInfo.description:
These very cowardly Pokémon join together and
use Reflect to protect their nest.

fullCardInfo.name: Ledian
fullCardInfo.description:
It flies through the night sky, sprinkling sparkly
dust. According to some, if that dust sticks to
you, good things will happen to you.

attacks.name: Swift
attacks.effect: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness or by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Shuckle
fullCardInfo.description:
The berries stored in its vaselike shell eventually
become a thick, pulpy juice.

attacks.name: Guard Press
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes −20 damage from attacks.

fullCardInfo.name: Roselia
fullCardInfo.description:
It uses the different poisons in each hand
separately when it attacks. The stronger its
aroma, the healthier it is.

attacks.name: Trip Over
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

fullCardInfo.name: Cacnea
fullCardInfo.description:
It lives in arid locations. Its yellow flowers bloom
once a year.

fullCardInfo.name: Cacturne
fullCardInfo.description:
It lives in deserts. It becomes active at night
when it hunts for prey exhausted from the
desert's heat.

attacks.name: Spike Rend
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon has damage on it, this attack does 60 more damage.

fullCardInfo.name: Chespin
fullCardInfo.description:
Eating lots of berries makes the bark shell that
covers Chespin's head and body grow sturdier.

fullCardInfo.name: Quilladin
fullCardInfo.description:
It can send a trailer flying with a tackle. It's numb
to pain, so even violent collisions don't faze it.

fullCardInfo.name: Chesnaught
fullCardInfo.description:
Its gentle disposition would have it avoid
conflict, but it gives opponents a thrashing with
its thorned fists when the situation calls for it.

attacks.name: Needle Lariat
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, if this Pokémon is damaged by an attack, do 80 damage to the Attacking Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Spewpa
fullCardInfo.description:
Spewpa doesn't live in a fixed location. It roams
where it pleases across the fields and mountains,
building up the energy it needs to evolve.

attacks.name: Signs of Evolution
attacks.effect: Put a random card that evolves from Spewpa from your deck into your hand.

fullCardInfo.name: Eldegoss
fullCardInfo.description:
The cotton on the head of this Pokémon can be
spun into a glossy, gorgeous yarn—a Galar
regional specialty.

attacks.name: Float Up
attacks.effect: You may shuffle this Pokémon and all attached cards into your deck.

fullCardInfo.name: Alolan Marowak
fullCardInfo.description:
The cursed flames that light up the bone carried
by this Pokémon are said to cause both mental
and physical pain that will never fade.

attacks.name: Fiery Bone
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

fullCardInfo.name: Reshiram
fullCardInfo.description:
When Reshiram's tail flares, the heat energy moves
the atmosphere and changes the world's weather.

attacks.name: Blue Flare
attacks.effect: Discard 2 {R} Energy from this Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Pyroar
fullCardInfo.description:
The females of a pride work together to bring
down prey. It's thanks to them that their pride
doesn't starve.

fullCardInfo.name: Raboot
fullCardInfo.description:
While it prides itself on its varied kicking moves,
it can also deliver powerful headbutts once its
flames have heated up its forehead.

fullCardInfo.name: Hearthflame Mask Ogerpon
fullCardInfo.description:
In this form, it draws on the power of fire.
It spears its enemies with thorn-covered ivy.

attacks.name: Hearthflame Dance
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, take 2 {R} Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Alolan Vulpix
fullCardInfo.description:
In hot weather, this Pokémon makes ice shards
with its six tails and sprays them around to cool
itself off.

attacks.name: Binding Snow
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, they can't take any Energy from their Energy Zone to attach to their Active Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Galarian Mr. Mime
fullCardInfo.description:
It can radiate chilliness from the bottoms of its
feet. It'll spend the whole day tap-dancing on a
frozen floor.

fullCardInfo.name: Galarian Mr. Rime
fullCardInfo.description:
Its amusing movements make it very popular.
It releases its psychic power from the pattern
on its belly.

attacks.name: Triple Spin
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 60 damage for each heads.

fullCardInfo.name: Delibird
fullCardInfo.description:
It always carries its food with it, wherever it goes.
If attacked, it throws its food at the opponent.

attacks.name: Box of Surprises
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 70 damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon. If tails, heal 30 damage from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Mudkip
fullCardInfo.description:
When it uses its large tail fin, it picks up speed
rapidly in the water. It is strong in spite of its
small size.

fullCardInfo.name: Swampert
fullCardInfo.description:
Swampert's arms are rock-hard. With one swing,
they can batter down Swampert's foes. This
Pokémon makes its nest on beautiful beaches.

attacks.name: Large Whirlpool
attacks.effect: Discard a random Energy from among the Energy attached to all Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's).

fullCardInfo.name: Vanillite
fullCardInfo.description:
Supposedly, this Pokémon was born from an
icicle. It spews out freezing air at −58 degrees
Fahrenheit to make itself more comfortable.

fullCardInfo.name: Vanillish
fullCardInfo.description:
It blasts enemies with cold air reaching −148
degrees Fahrenheit, freezing them solid. But it
spares their lives afterward—it's a kind Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Cryogonal
fullCardInfo.description:
Cryogonal uses its chains of ice to constrict its
opponents and then flash-freezes them where
they stand.

attacks.name: Frozen Lock
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, they can't play any Item cards from their hand.

fullCardInfo.name: Chewtle
fullCardInfo.description:
A popular game among children is to lift up sticks
that Chewtle have bitten onto to see whose
Chewtle can hang on the longest.

fullCardInfo.name: Cramorant
fullCardInfo.description:
Cramorant takes advantage of Arrokuda's frantic
thrashing to help spit Arrokuda out at high speed.

attacks.name: Spit-Out Shot
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Arrokuda
fullCardInfo.description:
After it's eaten its fill, its movements become
extremely sluggish. That's when Cramorant
swallows it up.

fullCardInfo.name: Barraskewda
fullCardInfo.description:
It spins its tail fins to propel itself, surging
forward at speeds of over 100 knots before
ramming prey and spearing into them.

fullCardInfo.name: Pikachu
fullCardInfo.description:
When several of these Pokémon gather, their
electricity can build and cause lightning storms.

attacks.name: Thunder Jolt
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

fullCardInfo.name: Plusle
fullCardInfo.description:
This Pokémon absorbs electricity from telephone
poles. It shorts out the electricity stored inside its
body to create noises.

attacks.name: Electric Tail
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

fullCardInfo.name: Minun
fullCardInfo.description:
Its dislike of water makes it take shelter under
the eaves of houses in rain. It uses pom-poms
made of sparks for cheering.

attacks.name: Buddy Spark
attacks.effect: If Plusle is on your Bench, this attack also does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Toxel
fullCardInfo.description:
This selfish, attention-seeking Pokémon stores
poison and electricity in two different sacs inside
its body.

fullCardInfo.name: Tadbulb
fullCardInfo.description:
It floats using the electricity stored in its body.
When thunderclouds are around, Tadbulb will
float higher off the ground.

attacks.name: Charge
attacks.effect: Take a {L} Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to this Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Bellibolt
fullCardInfo.description:
What appear to be eyeballs are actually organs
for discharging the electricity generated by
Bellibolt's belly-button dynamo.

fullCardInfo.name: Galarian Ponyta
fullCardInfo.description:
This Pokémon will look into your eyes and read
the contents of your heart. If it finds evil there,
it promptly hides away.

fullCardInfo.name: Galarian Rapidash
fullCardInfo.description:
Brave and prideful, this Pokémon dashes airily
through the forest, its steps aided by the psychic
power stored in the fur on its fetlocks.

attacks.name: Photon Laser
attacks.effect: If you have 5 or more {P} Energy in play, this attack does 60 more damage.

fullCardInfo.name: Wobbuffet
fullCardInfo.description:
To keep its pitch-black tail hidden, it lives quietly
in the darkness. It is never first to attack.

attacks.name: Flip Over
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

fullCardInfo.name: Snubbull
fullCardInfo.description:
Its growls make its opponents uneasy. This
laid-back Pokémon tends to sleep half the day.

fullCardInfo.name: Ralts
fullCardInfo.description:
It is highly attuned to the emotions of people
and Pokémon. It hides if it senses hostility.

attacks.name: Confuse Ray
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

fullCardInfo.name: Kirlia
fullCardInfo.description:
The cheerful spirit of its Trainer gives it energy
for its psychokinetic power. It spins and dances
when happy.

attacks.name: Psybeam
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

fullCardInfo.name: Gardevoir
fullCardInfo.description:
It unleashes psychokinetic energy at full power
when protecting a Trainer it has bonded closely
with.

attacks.name: Psy Turbo
attacks.effect: Take 2 {P} Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to 1 of your Benched {P} Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Litwick
fullCardInfo.description:
While shining a light and pretending to be a
guide, it leeches off the life-force of any who
follow it.

fullCardInfo.name: Lampent
fullCardInfo.description:
The spirits it absorbs fuel its baleful fire. It hangs
around hospitals waiting for people to pass on.

fullCardInfo.name: Pumpkaboo
fullCardInfo.description:
The light that streams out from the holes in the
pumpkin can hypnotize and control the people
and Pokémon that see it.

fullCardInfo.name: Gourgeist
fullCardInfo.description:
In the darkness of a new-moon night, Gourgeist
will come knocking. Whoever answers the door
will be swept off to the afterlife.

attacks.name: Soul Shot
attacks.effect: Discard a card from your hand. If you can't, this attack does nothing.

fullCardInfo.name: Sinistea
fullCardInfo.description:
Sinistea gets into your body when you drink it,
and then it steals your vitality from within. It also
tastes awful.

attacks.name: Hide
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from—and effects of—attacks done to this Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Indeedee
fullCardInfo.description:
This Pokémon never leaves its Trainer's side.
It predicts their actions with its psychic power
and takes care of their day-to-day needs.

attacks.name: Zen Shard
attacks.effect: This attack does 70 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Sandshrew
fullCardInfo.description:
No matter how high a place it falls from, this
Pokémon can save itself by rolling into a ball
and bouncing.

attacks.name: Double Scratch
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.

fullCardInfo.name: Sandslash
fullCardInfo.description:
The spikes on its body are made up of its
hardened hide. It rolls up and attacks foes
with its spikes.

attacks.name: Fury Swipes
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.

fullCardInfo.name: Machop
fullCardInfo.description:
Always brimming with power, it passes time by
lifting boulders. Doing so makes it even stronger.

attacks.name: Shatter
attacks.effect: Discard a Stadium in play.

fullCardInfo.name: Meditite
fullCardInfo.description:
It meditates to heighten its inner energy and to
float in the air. It eats one berry a day.

fullCardInfo.name: Medicham
fullCardInfo.description:
It elegantly avoids attacks with dance-like
steps, then launches a devastating blow in
the same motion.

attacks.name: Psykick
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has any {P} Energy attached, this attack does 50 more damage.

fullCardInfo.name: Boldore
fullCardInfo.description:
It relies on sound in order to monitor what's in its
vicinity. When angered, it will attack without ever
changing the direction it's facing.

attacks.name: Megaton Cannon
attacks.effect: This attack does 140 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

fullCardInfo.name: Drilbur
fullCardInfo.description:
It makes its way swiftly through the soil by putting
both claws together and rotating at high speed.

fullCardInfo.name: Tyrunt
fullCardInfo.description:
This Pokémon is selfish and likes to be pampered.
It can also inflict grievous wounds on its Trainer
just by playing around.

fullCardInfo.name: Passimian
fullCardInfo.description:
The boss chooses 10 members of the group to
go out hunting. The hunting party will evenly split
the food they find with the rest of the group.

attacks.name: Fling
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Alolan Persian
fullCardInfo.description:
This Pokémon is one tough opponent. Not only
does it have formidable physical abilities, but it's
also not above fighting dirty.

fullCardInfo.name: Alolan Grimer
fullCardInfo.description:
Alolan Grimer will gladly gobble up any kind
of trash, making it a widely used and popular
Pokémon at garbage disposal facilities.

fullCardInfo.name: Galarian Zigzagoon
fullCardInfo.description:
Thought to be the oldest form of Zigzagoon,
it moves in zigzags and wreaks havoc upon
its surroundings.

fullCardInfo.name: Galarian Linoone
fullCardInfo.description:
This very aggressive Pokémon will recklessly
challenge opponents stronger than itself.

fullCardInfo.name: Stunky
fullCardInfo.description:
The foul fluid from its rear is so revolting that it
can make people feel queasy up to a mile and a
quarter away.

fullCardInfo.name: Skuntank
fullCardInfo.description:
It attacks by spraying a repugnant fluid from its
tail, but the stench dulls after a few squirts.

attacks.name: Smokescreen Shot
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, if the Defending Pokémon tries to use an attack, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack doesn't happen.

fullCardInfo.name: Liepard
fullCardInfo.description:
Don't be fooled by its gorgeous fur and elegant
figure. This is a moody and vicious Pokémon.

attacks.name: Fasten Claws
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

fullCardInfo.name: Scraggy
fullCardInfo.description:
It will abruptly headbutt anyone that makes eye
contact with it. Its skull is extremely hard.

fullCardInfo.name: Yveltal
fullCardInfo.description:
When its life comes to an end, it absorbs
the life energy of every living thing
and turns into a cocoon once more.

attacks.name: Evil Crash
attacks.effect: Discard a random Energy from both Active Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Galarian Meowth
fullCardInfo.description:
These daring Pokémon have coins on their
foreheads. Darker coins are harder, and harder
coins garner more respect among Meowth.

fullCardInfo.name: Galarian Perrserker
fullCardInfo.description:
After many battles, it evolved dangerous
claws that come together to form daggers
when extended.

abilities.name: Dig Up
abilities.effect: Once during your turn, when you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may put 2 random Pokémon Tool cards from your discard pile into your hand.

fullCardInfo.name: Mawile
fullCardInfo.description:
It chomps with its gaping mouth. Its huge jaws are
actually steel horns that have been transformed.

fullCardInfo.name: Excadrill
fullCardInfo.description:
Its drill, which has evolved into steel, is strong
enough to bore through iron plates. This
Pokémon is a great help in tunnel construction.

fullCardInfo.name: Galarian Stunfisk
fullCardInfo.description:
Its conspicuous lips lure prey in as it lies in wait in
the mud. When prey gets close, Stunfisk clamps
its jagged steel fins down on them.

attacks.name: Snapping Trap
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot when your opponent's Active Pokémon retreats, this attack does 40 damage to the new Active Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Honedge
fullCardInfo.description:
The blue eye on the sword's handguard is the
true body of Honedge. With its old cloth, it
drains people's lives away.

fullCardInfo.name: Doublade
fullCardInfo.description:
The two swords employ a strategy of rapidly
alternating between offense and defense to
bring down their prey.

attacks.name: Dual Blades
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 40 damage for each heads.

fullCardInfo.name: Bagon
fullCardInfo.description:
Its belief that it will be able to fly one day is
apparently the influence of information carried
in its genes.

fullCardInfo.name: Shelgon
fullCardInfo.description:
Its armored body makes all attacks bounce off.
The armor is too tough, however, making it heavy
and somewhat sluggish.

fullCardInfo.name: Meowth
fullCardInfo.description:
It loves things that sparkle. When it sees a shiny
object, the gold coin on its head shines, too.

abilities.name: Carefree Steps
abilities.effect: If any damage is done to this Pokémon by attacks, flip a coin. If heads, prevent that damage.

attacks.name: Feelin' Fine
attacks.effect: Draw a card.

fullCardInfo.name: Persian
fullCardInfo.description:
It has a vicious temperament. Beware if it raises
its tail straight up. This is a signal that it is about
to pounce and bite.

fullCardInfo.name: Sentret
fullCardInfo.description:
If it encounters a foe, it will stand up on its tail,
making itself look bigger than usual to intimidate
them.

attacks.name: Tail Rap
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.

fullCardInfo.name: Smeargle
fullCardInfo.description:
The color of the mysterious fluid secreted from
the tip of the tail is predetermined for each
Smeargle.

abilities.name: Portrait
abilities.effect: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may look at a random Supporter card from your opponent's hand. Use the effect of that card as the effect of this Ability.

fullCardInfo.name: Taillow
fullCardInfo.description:
It dislikes cold seasons. They migrate to other
lands in search of warmth, flying over 180 miles
a day.

fullCardInfo.name: Slakoth
fullCardInfo.description:
The way Slakoth lolls around makes anyone who
watches it feel like doing the same.

attacks.name: Yawn
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

fullCardInfo.name: Vigoroth
fullCardInfo.description:
It is always hungry because it won't stop
rampaging. Even while it is eating, it can't
keep still.

attacks.name: Best Punch
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

fullCardInfo.name: Slaking
fullCardInfo.description:
This Pokémon lives lying on its side. It only
rolls over and moves when there is no more
grass to eat.

abilities.name: Snoozing Habit
abilities.effect: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, whenever you attach an Energy from your Energy Zone to it, it is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Knuckle Sandwich
attacks.effect: Discard a random Energy from this Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Spinda
fullCardInfo.description:
Each Spinda's spot pattern is different. With its
stumbling movements, it evades opponents'
attacks brilliantly!

attacks.name: Teetering Steps
attacks.effect: This Pokémon is now Confused.

fullCardInfo.name: Bunnelby
fullCardInfo.description:
It's very sensitive to danger. The sound of
Corviknight's flapping will have Bunnelby
digging a hole to hide underground in moments.

fullCardInfo.name: Diggersby
fullCardInfo.description:
The fur on its belly retains heat exceptionally
well. People used to make heavy winter clothing
from fur shed by this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Earthquake
attacks.effect: This attack also does 10 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Furfrou
fullCardInfo.description:
Left alone, its fur will grow longer and longer,
but it will only allow someone it trusts to cut it.

abilities.name: Fur Coat
abilities.effect: This Pokémon takes −20 damage from attacks.

fullCardInfo.name: Tandemaus
fullCardInfo.description:
The pair sticks together no matter what. They
split any food they find exactly in half and then
eat it together.

attacks.name: Flock
attacks.effect: Put 3 random cards from among Tandemaus and Maushold from your deck onto your Bench.

fullCardInfo.name: Maushold
fullCardInfo.description:
The larger pair protects the little ones during
battles. When facing strong opponents, the
whole group will join the fight.

attacks.name: Family Beatdown
attacks.effect: Flip a coin for each Tandemaus and Maushold you have in play. This attack does 60 damage for each heads.

fullCardInfo.name: Jaw Fossil
fullCardInfo.effect:
Play this card as if it were a 40-HP Basic {C} Pokémon.
At any time during your turn, you may discard this card from play.
This card can't retreat.

fullCardInfo.name: Lucky Ice Pop
fullCardInfo.effect: Heal 20 damage from your Active Pokémon. If you healed any damage in this way, flip a coin. If heads, put this Lucky Ice Pop into your hand instead of the discard pile.

fullCardInfo.name: Sail Fossil
fullCardInfo.effect:
Play this card as if it were a 40-HP Basic {C} Pokémon.
At any time during your turn, you may discard this card from play.
This card can't retreat.

fullCardInfo.name: Protective Poncho
fullCardInfo.effect: As long as the Pokémon this card is attached to is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to that Pokémon by your opponent's attacks and Abilities.

fullCardInfo.name: Diantha
fullCardInfo.effect: Heal 90 damage from 1 of your {P} Pokémon that has 2 or more {P} Energy attached. If you healed any damage in this way, discard 2 {P} Energy from that Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Starting Plains
fullCardInfo.effect: Each Basic Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent's) gets +20 HP.

fullCardInfo.name: Reshiram
fullCardInfo.description:
When Reshiram's tail flares, the heat energy moves
the atmosphere and changes the world's weather.

attacks.name: Blue Flare
attacks.effect: Discard 2 {R} Energy from this Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Cramorant
fullCardInfo.description:
Cramorant takes advantage of Arrokuda's frantic
thrashing to help spit Arrokuda out at high speed.

attacks.name: Spit-Out Shot
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Slakoth
fullCardInfo.description:
The way Slakoth lolls around makes anyone who
watches it feel like doing the same.

attacks.name: Yawn
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

abilities.name: Soothing Wind
abilities.effect: Each of your Pokémon that has any Energy attached recovers from all Special Conditions and can't be affected by any Special Conditions.

attacks.name: Energized Leaves
attacks.effect: If the amount of Energy attached to both Active Pokémon is 5 or more, this attack does 60 more damage.

fullCardInfo.name: Omastar
fullCardInfo.description:
Weighed down by a large and heavy shell,
Omastar couldn't move very fast. Some say it
went extinct because it was unable to catch food.

attacks.name: Ancient Whirlpool
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't attack.

fullCardInfo.name: Latios
fullCardInfo.description:
It understands human speech and is highly
intelligent. It is a tender Pokémon that
dislikes fighting.

abilities.name: Fantastical Floating
abilities.effect: If you have Latias in play, this Pokémon has no Retreat Cost.

attacks.name: Luster Purge
attacks.effect: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Bulbasaur
fullCardInfo.description: There is a plant seed on its back right from the day this Pokémon is born. The seed slowly grows larger.

fullCardInfo.name: Ivysaur
fullCardInfo.description: When the bulb on its back grows large, it appears to lose the ability to stand on its hind legs.

fullCardInfo.name: Venusaur
fullCardInfo.description: Its plant blooms when it is absorbing solar energy. It stays on the move to seek sunlight.

attacks.name: Mega Drain
attacks.effect: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Giant Bloom
attacks.effect: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Caterpie
fullCardInfo.description: For protection, it releases a horrible stench from the antenna on its head to drive away enemies.

attacks.name: Find a Friend
attacks.effect: Put 1 random {G} Pokémon from your deck into your hand.

fullCardInfo.name: Metapod
fullCardInfo.description: It is waiting for the moment to evolve. At this stage, it can only harden, so it remains motionless to avoid attack.

fullCardInfo.name: Butterfree
fullCardInfo.description: In battle, it flaps its wings at great speed to release highly toxic dust into the air.

abilities.name: Powder Heal
abilities.effect: Once during your turn, you may heal 20 damage from each of your Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Weedle
fullCardInfo.description: Often found in forests and grasslands. It has a sharp, toxic barb of around two inches on top of its head.

fullCardInfo.name: Kakuna
fullCardInfo.description: Almost incapable of moving, this Pokémon can only harden its shell to protect itself when it is in danger.

fullCardInfo.name: Beedrill
fullCardInfo.description: It has three poisonous stingers on its forelegs and its tail. They are used to jab its enemy repeatedly.

fullCardInfo.name: Oddish
fullCardInfo.description: If exposed to moonlight, it starts to move. It roams far and wide at night to scatter its seeds.

fullCardInfo.name: Gloom
fullCardInfo.description: Its pistils exude an incredibly foul odor. The horrid stench can cause fainting at a distance of 1.25 miles.

fullCardInfo.name: Vileplume
fullCardInfo.description: It has the world's largest petals. With every step, the petals shake out heavy clouds of toxic pollen.

attacks.name: Soothing Scent
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

fullCardInfo.name: Venonat
fullCardInfo.description: Poison oozes from all over its body. It catches small bug Pokémon at night that are attracted by light.

fullCardInfo.name: Venomoth
fullCardInfo.description: The wings are covered with dustlike scales. Every time it flaps its wings, it looses highly toxic dust.

attacks.name: Poison Powder
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

fullCardInfo.name: Bellsprout
fullCardInfo.description: Even though its body is extremely skinny, it is blindingly fast when catching its prey.

fullCardInfo.name: Weepinbell
fullCardInfo.description: The leafy parts act as cutters for slashing foes. It spits a fluid that dissolves everything.

fullCardInfo.name: Exeggcute
fullCardInfo.description: Though it may look like it's just a bunch of eggs, it's a proper Pokémon. Exeggcute communicates with others of its kind via telepathy, apparently.

fullCardInfo.name: Tangela
fullCardInfo.description: Hidden beneath a tangle of vines that grows nonstop even if the vines are torn off, this Pokémon's true appearance remains a mystery.

attacks.name: Absorb
attacks.effect: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Scyther
fullCardInfo.description: It slashes through grass with its sharp scythes, moving too fast for the human eye to track.

fullCardInfo.name: Pinsir
fullCardInfo.description: These Pokémon judge one another based on pincers. Thicker, more impressive pincers make for more popularity with the opposite gender.

attacks.name: Double Horn
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 50 damage for each heads.

fullCardInfo.name: Cottonee
fullCardInfo.description: It shoots cotton from its body to protect itself. If it gets caught up in hurricane-strength winds, it can get sent to the other side of the Earth.

fullCardInfo.name: Whimsicott
fullCardInfo.description: It scatters cotton all over the place as a prank. If it gets wet, it'll become too heavy to move and have no choice but to answer for its mischief.

fullCardInfo.name: Petilil
fullCardInfo.description: The leaves on its head grow right back even if they fall out. These bitter leaves refresh those who eat them.

attacks.name: Blot
attacks.effect: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Lilligant
fullCardInfo.description: The fragrance of the garland on its head has a relaxing effect, but taking care of it is very difficult.

attacks.name: Leaf Supply
attacks.effect: Take a {G} Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to 1 of your Benched {G} Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Charmander
fullCardInfo.description: It has a preference for hot things. When it rains, steam is said to spout from the tip of its tail.

attacks.name: Ember
attacks.effect: Discard a {R} Energy from this Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Charmeleon
fullCardInfo.description: It has a barbaric nature. In battle, it whips its fiery tail around and slashes away with sharp claws.

attacks.name: Crimson Storm
attacks.effect: Discard 2 {R} Energy from this Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Growlithe
fullCardInfo.description: It has a brave and trustworthy nature. It fearlessly stands up to bigger and stronger foes.

fullCardInfo.name: Arcanine
fullCardInfo.description: An ancient picture scroll shows that people were captivated by its movement as it ran through prairies.

attacks.name: Heat Tackle
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.

fullCardInfo.name: Ponyta
fullCardInfo.description: It can't run properly when it's newly born. As it races around with others of its kind, its legs grow stronger.

fullCardInfo.name: Rapidash
fullCardInfo.description: This Pokémon can be seen galloping through fields at speeds of up to 150 mph, its fiery mane fluttering in the wind.

attacks.name: Inferno Dance
