attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. Take an amount of {R} Energy from your Energy Zone equal to the number of heads and attach it to your Benched {R} Pokémon in any way you like.

fullCardInfo.name: Heatmor
fullCardInfo.description: There's a hole in its tail that allows it to draw in the air it needs to keep its fire burning. If the hole gets blocked, this Pokémon will fall ill.

fullCardInfo.name: Salandit
fullCardInfo.description: It taunts its prey and lures them into narrow, rocky areas where it then sprays them with toxic gas to make them dizzy and take them down.

fullCardInfo.name: Sizzlipede
fullCardInfo.description: It stores flammable gas in its body and uses it to generate heat. The yellow sections on its belly get particularly hot.

fullCardInfo.name: Centiskorch
fullCardInfo.description: When it heats up, its body temperature reaches about 1,500 degrees Fahrenheit. It lashes its body like a whip and launches itself at enemies.

attacks.name: Fire Blast
attacks.effect: Discard a {R} Energy from this Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Squirtle
fullCardInfo.description: When it retracts its long neck into its shell, it squirts out water with vigorous force.

fullCardInfo.name: Wartortle
fullCardInfo.description: It is recognized as a symbol of longevity. If its shell has algae on it, that Wartortle is very old.

attacks.name: Hydro Bazooka
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has at least 2 extra {W} Energy attached, this attack does 60 more damage.

fullCardInfo.name: Psyduck
fullCardInfo.description: It is constantly wracked by a headache. When the headache turns intense, it begins using mysterious powers.

attacks.name: Headache
attacks.effect: Your opponent can't use any Supporter cards from their hand during their next turn.

fullCardInfo.name: Golduck
fullCardInfo.description: When it swims at full speed using its long, webbed limbs, its forehead somehow begins to glow.

fullCardInfo.name: Poliwag
fullCardInfo.description: For Poliwag, swimming is easier than walking. The swirl pattern on its belly is actually part of the Pokémon's innards showing through the skin.

fullCardInfo.name: Tentacool
fullCardInfo.description: Tentacool is not a particularly strong swimmer. It drifts across the surface of shallow seas as it searches for prey.

fullCardInfo.name: Tentacruel
fullCardInfo.description: When the red orbs on Tentacruel's head glow brightly, watch out. The Pokémon is about to fire off a burst of ultrasonic waves.

attacks.name: Poison Tentacles
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

fullCardInfo.name: Seel
fullCardInfo.description: Thanks to its thick fat, cold seas don't bother it at all, but it gets tired pretty easily in warm waters.

fullCardInfo.name: Shellder
fullCardInfo.description: It is encased in a shell that is harder than diamond. Inside, however, it is surprisingly tender.

fullCardInfo.name: Cloyster
fullCardInfo.description: Cloyster that live in seas with harsh tidal currents grow large, sharp spikes on their shells.

abilities.name: Shell Armor
abilities.effect: This Pokémon takes -10 damage from attacks.

fullCardInfo.name: Krabby
fullCardInfo.description: It can be found near the sea. The large pincers grow back if they are torn out of their sockets.

fullCardInfo.name: Kingler
fullCardInfo.description: Its large and hard pincer has 10,000-horsepower strength. However, being so big, it is unwieldy to move.

attacks.name: KO Crab
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. If both of them are heads, this attack does 80 more damage.

fullCardInfo.name: Seaking
fullCardInfo.description: In autumn, its body becomes more fatty in preparing to propose to a mate. It takes on beautiful colors.

attacks.name: Horn Hazard
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

fullCardInfo.name: Staryu
fullCardInfo.description: If you visit a beach at the end of summer, you'll be able to see groups of Staryu lighting up in a steady rhythm.

fullCardInfo.name: Starmie
fullCardInfo.description: This Pokémon has an organ known as its core. The organ glows in seven colors when Starmie is unleashing its potent psychic powers.

fullCardInfo.name: Magikarp
fullCardInfo.description: An underpowered, pathetic Pokémon. It may jump high on rare occasions but never more than seven feet.

fullCardInfo.name: Lapras
fullCardInfo.description: A smart and kindhearted Pokémon, it glides across the surface of the sea while its beautiful song echoes around it.

attacks.name: Hydro Pump
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has at least 3 extra {W} Energy attached, this attack does 70 more damage.

fullCardInfo.name: Omanyte
fullCardInfo.description: Because some Omanyte manage to escape after being restored or are released into the wild by people, this species is becoming a problem.

fullCardInfo.name: Swanna
fullCardInfo.description: Despite their elegant appearance, they can flap their wings strongly and fly for thousands of miles.

fullCardInfo.name: Froakie
fullCardInfo.description: It secretes flexible bubbles from its chest and back. The bubbles reduce the damage it would otherwise take when attacked.

fullCardInfo.name: Pyukumuku
fullCardInfo.description: It lives in warm, shallow waters. If it encounters a foe, it will spit out its internal organs as a means to punch them.

fullCardInfo.name: Bruxish
fullCardInfo.description: It grinds its teeth with great force to stimulate its brain. It fires the psychic energy created by this process from the protuberance on its head.

attacks.name: Second Strike
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon has damage on it, this attack does 60 more damage.

fullCardInfo.name: Snom
fullCardInfo.description: It eats snow that has accumulated on the ground. It prefers soft, freshly fallen snow, so it will eat its way up a mountain, aiming for the peak.

fullCardInfo.name: Frosmoth
fullCardInfo.description: Frosmoth senses air currents with its antennae. It sends its scales drifting on frigid air, making them fall like snow.

attacks.name: Powder Snow
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Circle Circuit
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage for each of your Benched {L} Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Magnemite
fullCardInfo.description: The electromagnetic waves emitted by the units at the sides of its head expel antigravity, which allows it to float.

fullCardInfo.name: Voltorb
fullCardInfo.description: It rolls to move. If the ground is uneven, a sudden jolt from hitting a bump can cause it to explode.

fullCardInfo.name: Electrode
fullCardInfo.description: The more energy it charges up, the faster it gets. But this also makes it more likely to explode.

fullCardInfo.name: Electabuzz
fullCardInfo.description: Many power plants keep Ground-type Pokémon around as a defense against Electabuzz that come seeking electricity.

attacks.name: Thunder Punch
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 more damage. If tails, this Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.

fullCardInfo.name: Zapdos
fullCardInfo.description: This Pokémon has complete control over electricity. There are tales of Zapdos nesting in the dark depths of pitch-black thunderclouds.

attacks.name: Raging Thunder
attacks.effect: This attack also does 30 damage to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Thundering Hurricane
attacks.effect: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 50 damage for each heads.

fullCardInfo.name: Blitzle
fullCardInfo.description: When thunderclouds cover the sky, it will appear. It can catch lightning with its mane and store the electricity.

fullCardInfo.name: Zebstrika
fullCardInfo.description: When this ill-tempered Pokémon runs wild, it shoots lightning from its mane in all directions.

attacks.name: Thunder Spear
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Tynamo
fullCardInfo.description: While one alone doesn't have much power, a chain of many Tynamo can be as powerful as lightning.

fullCardInfo.name: Eelektrik
fullCardInfo.description: They coil around foes and shock them with electricity-generating organs that seem simply to be circular patterns.

fullCardInfo.name: Helioptile
fullCardInfo.description: When spread, the frills on its head act like solar panels, generating the power behind this Pokémon's electric moves.

fullCardInfo.name: Heliolisk
fullCardInfo.description: One Heliolisk basking in the sun with its frill outspread is all it would take to produce enough electricity to power a city.

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 more damage.

fullCardInfo.name: Pincurchin
fullCardInfo.description: This Pokémon generates electricity when it digests food. It uses its five hard teeth to scrape seaweed off surfaces and eat it.

attacks.name: Thunder Shock
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

fullCardInfo.name: Clefairy
fullCardInfo.description: It is said that happiness will come to those who see a gathering of Clefairy dancing under a full moon.

fullCardInfo.name: Abra
fullCardInfo.description: This Pokémon uses its psychic powers while it sleeps. The contents of Abra's dreams affect the powers that the Pokémon wields.

attacks.name: Teleport
attacks.effect: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Kadabra
fullCardInfo.description: Using its psychic power, Kadabra levitates as it sleeps. It uses its springy tail as a pillow.

fullCardInfo.name: Slowpoke
fullCardInfo.description: It is incredibly slow and dopey. It takes five seconds for it to feel pain when under attack.

fullCardInfo.name: Slowbro
fullCardInfo.description: When a Slowpoke went hunting in the sea, its tail was bitten by a Shellder. That made it evolve into Slowbro.

fullCardInfo.name: Gastly
fullCardInfo.description: It wraps its opponent in its gas-like body, slowly weakening its prey by poisoning it through the skin.

fullCardInfo.name: Haunter
fullCardInfo.description: It likes to lurk in the dark and tap shoulders with a gaseous hand. Its touch causes endless shuddering.

abilities.name: Shadowy Spellbind
abilities.effect: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, your opponent can't use any Supporter cards from their hand.

fullCardInfo.name: Mr. Mime
fullCardInfo.description: The broadness of its hands may be no coincidence—many scientists believe its palms became enlarged specifically for pantomiming.

attacks.name: Barrier Attack
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes -20 damage from attacks.

fullCardInfo.name: Mewtwo
fullCardInfo.description: It was created by a scientist after years of horrific gene-splicing and DNA-engineering experiments.

attacks.name: Power Blast
attacks.effect: Discard 2 {P} Energy from this Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Ralts
fullCardInfo.description: The horns on its head provide a strong power that enables it to sense people's emotions.

fullCardInfo.name: Kirlia
fullCardInfo.description: It has a psychic power that enables it to distort the space around it and see into the future.

fullCardInfo.name: Woobat
fullCardInfo.description: While inside a cave, if you look up and see lots of heart-shaped marks lining the walls, it's evidence that Woobat live there.

fullCardInfo.name: Golett
fullCardInfo.description: They were sculpted from clay in ancient times. No one knows why, but some of them are driven to continually line up boulders.

fullCardInfo.name: Golurk
fullCardInfo.description: Artillery platforms built into the walls of ancient castles served as perches from which Golurk could fire energy beams.

attacks.name: Double Lariat
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 100 damage for each heads.

fullCardInfo.name: Sandshrew
fullCardInfo.description: It loves to bathe in the grit of dry, sandy areas. By sand bathing, the Pokémon rids itself of dirt and moisture clinging to its body.

fullCardInfo.name: Sandslash
fullCardInfo.description: The drier the area Sandslash lives in, the harder and smoother the Pokémon's spikes will feel when touched.

fullCardInfo.name: Dugtrio
fullCardInfo.description: Its three heads bob separately up and down to loosen the soil nearby, making it easier for it to burrow.

attacks.name: Dig
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage from—and effects of—attacks done to this Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Mankey
fullCardInfo.description: It lives in groups in the treetops. If it loses sight of its group, it becomes infuriated by its loneliness.

fullCardInfo.name: Machop
fullCardInfo.description: Its whole body is composed of muscles. Even though it's the size of a human child, it can hurl 100 grown-ups.

fullCardInfo.name: Geodude
fullCardInfo.description: Geodude that have lived a long life have had all their edges smoothed out until they're totally round. They also have a calm, quiet disposition.

fullCardInfo.name: Graveler
fullCardInfo.description: It climbs up cliffs as it heads toward the peak of a mountain. As soon as it reaches the summit, it rolls back down the way it came.

fullCardInfo.name: Onix
fullCardInfo.description: As it digs through the ground, it absorbs many hard objects. This is what makes its body so solid.

fullCardInfo.name: Cubone
fullCardInfo.description: When the memory of its departed mother brings it to tears, its cries echo mournfully within the skull it wears on its head.

attacks.name: Growl
attacks.effect: During your opponent’s next turn, attacks used by the Defending Pokémon do −20 damage.

fullCardInfo.name: Marowak
fullCardInfo.description: This Pokémon overcame its sorrow to evolve a sturdy new body. Marowak faces its opponents bravely, using a bone as a weapon.

attacks.name: Bonemerang
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 80 damage for each heads.

fullCardInfo.name: Hitmonlee
fullCardInfo.description: This amazing Pokémon has an awesome sense of balance. It can kick in succession from any position.

attacks.name: Stretch Kick
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Hitmonchan
fullCardInfo.description: Its punches slice the air. They are launched at such high speed, even a slight graze could cause a burn.

fullCardInfo.name: Rhyhorn
fullCardInfo.description: Strong, but not too bright, this Pokémon can shatter even a skyscraper with its charging tackles.

fullCardInfo.name: Kabuto
fullCardInfo.description: This species is almost entirely extinct. Kabuto molt every three days, making their shells harder and harder.

fullCardInfo.name: Mienfoo
fullCardInfo.description: In one minute, a well-trained Mienfoo can chop with its arms more than 100 times.

fullCardInfo.name: Mienshao
fullCardInfo.description: When Mienshao comes across a truly challenging opponent, it will lighten itself by biting off the fur on its arms.

fullCardInfo.name: Clobbopus
fullCardInfo.description: It's very curious, but its means of investigating things is to try to punch them with its tentacles. The search for food is what brings it onto land.

fullCardInfo.name: Grapploct
fullCardInfo.description: A body made up of nothing but muscle makes the grappling moves this Pokémon performs with its tentacles tremendously powerful.

attacks.name: Knock Back
attacks.effect: Switch out your opponent’s Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)

fullCardInfo.name: Ekans
fullCardInfo.description: By dislocating its jaw, it can swallow prey larger than itself. After a meal, it curls up and rests.

fullCardInfo.name: Arbok
fullCardInfo.description: The latest research has determined that there are over 20 possible arrangements of the patterns on its stomach.

attacks.name: Corner
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

fullCardInfo.name: Nidoran♀
fullCardInfo.description: Females are more sensitive to smells than males. While foraging, they'll use their whiskers to check wind direction and stay downwind of predators.

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.effect: Put 1 random Nidoran♂ from your deck onto your Bench.

fullCardInfo.name: Nidorino
fullCardInfo.description: With a horn that's harder than diamond, this Pokémon goes around shattering boulders as it searches for a moon stone.

fullCardInfo.name: Zubat
fullCardInfo.description: It emits ultrasonic waves from its mouth to check its surroundings. Even in tight caves, Zubat flies around with skill.

fullCardInfo.name: Golbat
fullCardInfo.description: It loves to drink other creatures' blood. It's said that if it finds others of its kind going hungry, it sometimes shares the blood it's gathered.

fullCardInfo.name: Muk
fullCardInfo.description: It's thickly covered with a filthy, vile sludge. It is so toxic, even its footprints contain poison.

attacks.name: Venoshock
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is Poisoned, this attack does 50 more damage.

fullCardInfo.name: Koffing
fullCardInfo.description: Its body is full of poisonous gas. It floats into garbage dumps, seeking out the fumes of raw, rotting trash.

fullCardInfo.name: Pawniard
fullCardInfo.description: Pawniard will fearlessly challenge even powerful foes. In a pinch, it will cling to opponents and pierce them with the blades all over its body.

fullCardInfo.name: Bisharp
fullCardInfo.description: This Pokémon commands a group of several Pawniard. Groups that are defeated in territorial disputes are absorbed by the winning side.

fullCardInfo.name: Dratini
fullCardInfo.description: It sheds many layers of skin as it grows larger. During this process, it is protected by a rapid waterfall.

fullCardInfo.name: Dragonair
fullCardInfo.description: They say that if it emits an aura from its whole body, the weather will begin to change instantly.

fullCardInfo.name: Pidgey
fullCardInfo.description: A common sight in forests and woods. It flaps its wings at ground level to kick up blinding sand.

fullCardInfo.name: Pidgeotto
fullCardInfo.description: The claws on its feet are well developed. It can carry prey such as an Exeggcute to its nest over 60 miles away.

fullCardInfo.name: Rattata
fullCardInfo.description: Its incisors grow continuously throughout its life. If its incisors get too long, this Pokémon becomes unable to eat, and it starves to death.

fullCardInfo.name: Raticate
fullCardInfo.description: People say that it fled from its enemies by using its small webbed hind feet to swim from island to island in Alola.

fullCardInfo.name: Spearow
fullCardInfo.description: Its reckless nature leads it to stand up to others—even large Pokémon—if it has to protect its territory.

fullCardInfo.name: Jigglypuff
fullCardInfo.description: When its huge eyes waver, it sings a mysteriously soothing melody that lulls its enemies to sleep.

fullCardInfo.name: Wigglytuff
fullCardInfo.description: It has a very fine fur. Take care not to make it angry, or it may inflate steadily and hit with a body slam.

attacks.name: Sleepy Song
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

fullCardInfo.name: Persian
fullCardInfo.description: Although its fur has many admirers, it is tough to raise as a pet because of its fickle meanness.

attacks.name: Shadow Claw
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, discard a random card from your opponent's hand.

fullCardInfo.name: Farfetch'd
fullCardInfo.description: The stalk this Pokémon carries in its wings serves as a sword to cut down opponents. In a dire situation, the stalk can also serve as food.

fullCardInfo.name: Doduo
fullCardInfo.description: A two-headed Pokémon that was discovered as a sudden mutation. It runs at a pace of over 60 miles per hour.

fullCardInfo.name: Dodrio
fullCardInfo.description: An enemy that takes its eyes off any of the three heads—even for a second—will get pecked severely.

fullCardInfo.name: Kangaskhan
fullCardInfo.description: Although it's carrying its baby in a pouch on its belly, Kangaskhan is swift on its feet. It intimidates its opponents with quick jabs.

attacks.name: Dizzy Punch
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.

fullCardInfo.name: Tauros
fullCardInfo.description: When Tauros begins whipping itself with its tails, it's a warning that the Pokémon is about to charge with astounding speed.

fullCardInfo.name: Eevee
fullCardInfo.description: Its ability to evolve into many forms allows it to adapt smoothly and perfectly to any environment.

fullCardInfo.name: Porygon
fullCardInfo.description: State-of-the-art technology was used to create Porygon. It was the first artificial Pokémon to be created via computer programming.

abilities.name: Data Scan
abilities.effect: Once during your turn, you may look at the top card of your deck.

fullCardInfo.name: Aerodactyl
fullCardInfo.description: This is a ferocious Pokémon from ancient times. Apparently even modern technology is incapable of producing a perfectly restored specimen.

attacks.name: Primal Wingbeat
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent shuffles their Active Pokémon into their deck.

fullCardInfo.name: Minccino
fullCardInfo.description: The way it brushes away grime with its tail can be helpful when cleaning. But its focus on spotlessness can make cleaning more of a hassle.

fullCardInfo.name: Cinccino
fullCardInfo.description: Its body secretes oil that this Pokémon spreads over its nest as a coating to protect it from dust. Cinccino won't tolerate even a speck of the stuff.

attacks.name: Do the Wave
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage for each of your Benched Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Wooloo
fullCardInfo.description: Its curly fleece is such an effective cushion that this Pokémon could fall off a cliff and stand right back up at the bottom, unharmed.

fullCardInfo.name: Dubwool
fullCardInfo.description: Weave a carpet from its springy wool, and you end up with something closer to a trampoline. You'll start to bounce the moment you set foot on it.

fullCardInfo.name: Helix Fossil
fullCardInfo.effect:
Play this card as if it were a 40-HP Basic {C} Pokémon.
At any time during your turn, you may discard this card from play.
This card can't retreat.

fullCardInfo.name: Dome Fossil
fullCardInfo.effect:
Play this card as if it were a 40-HP Basic {C} Pokémon.
At any time during your turn, you may discard this card from play.
This card can't retreat.

fullCardInfo.name: Old Amber
fullCardInfo.effect:
Play this card as if it were a 40-HP Basic {C} Pokémon.
At any time during your turn, you may discard this card from play.
This card can't retreat.

fullCardInfo.name: Misty
fullCardInfo.effect: Choose 1 of your {W} Pokémon, and flip a coin until you get tails. For each heads, take a {W} Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to that Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Giovanni
fullCardInfo.effect: During this turn, attacks used by your Pokémon do +10 damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Sabrina
fullCardInfo.effect: Switch out your opponent's Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)

fullCardInfo.name: Lt. Surge
fullCardInfo.effect: Move all {L} Energy from your Benched Pokémon to your Raichu, Electrode, or Electabuzz in the Active Spot.

fullCardInfo.name: Bulbasaur
fullCardInfo.description: There is a plant seed on its back right from the day this Pokémon is born. The seed slowly grows larger.

fullCardInfo.name: Alakazam
fullCardInfo.description: It has an incredibly high level of intelligence. Some say that Alakazam remembers everything that ever happens to it, from birth till death.

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Slowpoke
fullCardInfo.description: It is incredibly slow and dopey. It takes five seconds for it to feel pain when under attack.

fullCardInfo.name: Diglett
fullCardInfo.description: It lives about one yard underground, where it feeds on plant roots. It sometimes appears aboveground.

fullCardInfo.name: Cubone
fullCardInfo.description: When the memory of its departed mother brings it to tears, its cries echo mournfully within the skull it wears on its head.

attacks.name: Growl
attacks.effect: During your opponent’s next turn, attacks used by the Defending Pokémon do −20 damage.

fullCardInfo.name: Weezing
fullCardInfo.description: If one of the twin Koffing inflates, the other one deflates. It constantly mixes its poisonous gases.

abilities.name: Gas Leak
abilities.effect: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may make your opponent's Active Pokémon Poisoned.

fullCardInfo.name: Dragonite
fullCardInfo.description: It is said that somewhere in the ocean lies an island where these gather. Only they live there.

attacks.name: Draco Meteor
attacks.effect: 1 of your opponent's Pokémon is chosen at random 4 times. For each time a Pokémon was chosen, do 50 damage to it.

fullCardInfo.name: Pidgeot
fullCardInfo.description: When hunting, it skims the surface of water at high speed to pick off unwary prey such as Magikarp.

abilities.name: Drive Off
abilities.effect: Once during your turn, you may switch out your opponent's Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)

fullCardInfo.name: Meowth
fullCardInfo.description: All it does is sleep during the daytime. At night, it patrols its territory with its eyes aglow.

attacks.name: Pay Day
attacks.effect: Draw a card.

attacks.name: Psydrive
attacks.effect: Discard 2 {P} Energy from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Crimson Storm
attacks.effect: Discard 2 {R} Energy from this Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Wurmple
fullCardInfo.description:
It lives among the tall grass and in forests. It repels
attacks by raising up the spikes on its rear.

fullCardInfo.name: Cascoon
fullCardInfo.description:
It never forgets any attack it endured while in the
cocoon. After evolution, it seeks payback.

attacks.name: Harden
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks if that damage is 40 or less.

fullCardInfo.name: Seedot
fullCardInfo.description:
It attaches itself to a tree branch using the top of
its head. Strong winds can sometimes make it fall.

fullCardInfo.name: Nuzleaf
fullCardInfo.description:
They live in holes bored in large trees. The sound
of Nuzleaf's grass flute fills listeners with dread.

fullCardInfo.name: Shiftry
fullCardInfo.description:
It lives quietly in the deep forest. It is said to
create chilly winter winds with the fans it holds.

attacks.name: Nipping Cyclone
attacks.effect: Discard a random card from your opponent's hand.

fullCardInfo.name: Shroomish
fullCardInfo.description:
If it senses danger, it scatters spores from the top
of its head to protect itself.

fullCardInfo.name: Pansage
fullCardInfo.description:
It's good at finding berries and gathers them from
all over. It's kind enough to share them
with friends.

fullCardInfo.name: Simisage
fullCardInfo.description:
Ill tempered, it fights by swinging its barbed tail
around wildly. The leaf growing on its head is
very bitter.

attacks.name: Fury Swipes
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 40 damage for each heads.

fullCardInfo.name: Cottonee
fullCardInfo.description:
It shoots cotton from its body to protect itself.
If it gets caught up in hurricane-strength winds,
it can get sent to the other side of the Earth.

fullCardInfo.name: Petilil
fullCardInfo.description:
If the leaves on its head are pruned with
regularity, this Pokémon can be grown into
a fine plump shape.

fullCardInfo.name: Maractus
fullCardInfo.description:
Arid regions are their habitat. They move
rhythmically, making a sound similar to maracas.

attacks.name: Dazzle Dance
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

fullCardInfo.name: Virizion
fullCardInfo.description:
It darts around opponents with a flurry of quick
movements, slicing them up with its horns.

attacks.name: Sacred Sword
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Sacred Sword.

fullCardInfo.name: Skiddo
fullCardInfo.description:
As long as it has sunlight and water, Skiddo can
make energy with the leaves on its body, allowing
it to live on rocky mountains barren of food.

fullCardInfo.name: Gogoat
fullCardInfo.description:
These Pokémon live in herds in mountainous
places. The victor in their contests of clashing
horns will become the leader of the herd.

attacks.name: Double-Edge
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.

fullCardInfo.name: Phantump
fullCardInfo.description:
With a voice like a human child's, it cries out to
lure adults deep into the forest, getting them lost
among the trees.

fullCardInfo.name: Trevenant
fullCardInfo.description:
Small roots that extend from the tips of this
Pokémon's feet can tie into the trees of the
forest and give Trevenant control over them.

attacks.name: Horn Leech
attacks.effect: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Grookey
fullCardInfo.description:
It attacks with rapid beats of its stick.
As it strikes with amazing speed, it gets
more and more pumped.

fullCardInfo.name: Rillaboom
fullCardInfo.description:
The one with the best drumming techniques
becomes the boss of the troop. It has a gentle
disposition and values harmony among its group.

abilities.name: Captivating Rhythm
abilities.effect: Once during your turn, you may flip a coin. If heads, switch in 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon to the Active Spot.

fullCardInfo.name: Arcanine
fullCardInfo.description:
Its magnificent bark conveys a sense of majesty.
Anyone hearing it can't help but grovel before it.

attacks.name: Fire Fang
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

fullCardInfo.name: Ponyta
fullCardInfo.description:
If you've been accepted by Ponyta, its burning
mane is mysteriously no longer hot to the touch.

fullCardInfo.name: Torchic
fullCardInfo.description:
A fire burns inside, so it feels very warm to hug.
It launches fireballs of 1,800 degrees Fahrenheit.

fullCardInfo.name: Combusken
fullCardInfo.description:
During a battle, the hot flame in its body increases.
Its kicks have outstanding destructive power.

fullCardInfo.name: Mega Blaziken ex
fullCardInfo.description:
When facing a tough foe, it looses flames from
its wrists. Its powerful legs let it jump clear over
buildings.

attacks.name: Mega Burning
attacks.effect: Discard Fire{R} Energy from this Pokémon. Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

fullCardInfo.name: Darmanitan
fullCardInfo.description:
This Pokémon's power level rises along with the
temperature of its fire, which can reach 2,500
degrees Fahrenheit.

attacks.name: Darmani-Hands
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 more damage for each heads.

fullCardInfo.name: Heatmor
fullCardInfo.description:
A flame serves as its tongue, melting through the
hard shell of Durant so that Heatmor can devour
their insides.

attacks.name: Tongue Whip
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Pyroar
fullCardInfo.description:
The mane of a male Pyroar heats up to over
3,600 degrees Fahrenheit during battle. Merely
approaching it will cause severe burns.

attacks.name: Fire Blast
attacks.effect: Discard 2 {R} Energy from this Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Turtonator
fullCardInfo.description:
Eating sulfur in its volcanic habitat is what causes
explosive compounds to develop in its shell.
Its droppings are also dangerously explosive.

attacks.name: Shell Trap
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, if this Pokémon is damaged by an attack, do 20 damage to the Attacking Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Mega Gyarados ex
fullCardInfo.description:
Mega Evolution also affects its brain, leaving
no other function except its destructive instinct
to burn everything to cinders.

attacks.name: Mega Blaster
attacks.effect: Discard the top 3 cards of your opponent's deck.

fullCardInfo.name: Lombre
fullCardInfo.description:
It lives at the water's edge where it is sunny.
It sleeps on a bed of water grass by day and
becomes active at night.

fullCardInfo.name: Corphish
fullCardInfo.description:
It was originally a Pokémon from afar that escaped
to the wild. It can adapt to the dirtiest river.

fullCardInfo.name: Luvdisc
fullCardInfo.description:
During the spawning season, countless Luvdisc
congregate at coral reefs, turning the waters pink.

attacks.name: Paired Tackle
attacks.effect: If you have exactly 2, 4, or 6 cards in your hand, this attack does 30 more damage.

fullCardInfo.name: Panpour
fullCardInfo.description:
The water stored inside the tuft on its head is full of
nutrients. Plants that receive its water grow large.

fullCardInfo.name: Tympole
fullCardInfo.description:
It uses sound waves to communicate with
others of its kind. People and other Pokémon
species can't hear its cries of warning.

fullCardInfo.name: Palpitoad
fullCardInfo.description:
On occasion, their cries are sublimely pleasing to
the ear. Palpitoad with larger lumps on their
bodies can sing with a wider range of sounds.

fullCardInfo.name: Seismitoad
fullCardInfo.description:
This Pokémon is popular among the elderly,
who say the vibrations of its lumps are great
for massages.

attacks.name: Split Spiral Punch
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

fullCardInfo.name: Tirtouga
fullCardInfo.description:
Tirtouga is considered to be the ancestor of
many turtle Pokémon. It was restored to life
from a fossil.

fullCardInfo.name: Frillish
fullCardInfo.description:
Legend has it that the residents of a sunken
ancient city changed into these Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Bergmite
fullCardInfo.description:
They live in mountainous regions of frigid cold.
On rare occasions, they ride on the back of an
Avalugg to cross seas and move to new habitats.

fullCardInfo.name: Drednaw
fullCardInfo.description:
Drednaw lurks along the shoreline. When prey
come to drink water, Drednaw stretches its neck
out and chomps down on them.

attacks.name: Jaw Lock
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

fullCardInfo.name: Arrokuda
fullCardInfo.description:
It takes down prey by charging into them with
its hard, pointed jaw. But Arrokuda's eyesight is
poor, so this tactic has a low success rate.

fullCardInfo.name: Mareep
fullCardInfo.description:
Its fleece grows continually. In the summer,
the fleece is fully shed, but it grows back in
a week.

fullCardInfo.name: Ampharos
fullCardInfo.description:
When it gets dark, the light from its bright,
shiny tail can be seen from far away on the
ocean's surface.

attacks.name: Zapping Bullet
attacks.effect: 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon is chosen at random. This attack also does 20 damage to it.

fullCardInfo.name: Pachirisu
fullCardInfo.description:
A pair may be seen rubbing their cheek pouches
together in an effort to share stored electricity.

attacks.name: Attack the Wound
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon has damage on it, this attack does 30 more damage.

fullCardInfo.name: Joltik
fullCardInfo.description:
Joltik can be found clinging to other Pokémon.
It's soaking up static electricity because it can't
produce a charge on its own.

fullCardInfo.name: Galvantula
fullCardInfo.description:
It lays traps of electrified threads near the nests
of bird Pokémon, aiming to snare chicks that are
not yet good at flying.

fullCardInfo.name: Dedenne
fullCardInfo.description:
Dedenne emit electrical waves from the whiskers
on their cheeks to communicate with each other.
When low on electricity, they curl up and sleep.

attacks.name: Electric Nibbling
attacks.effect: Discard a {L} Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Yamper
fullCardInfo.description:
This gluttonous Pokémon only assists people with
their work because it wants treats. As it runs,
it crackles with electricity.

attacks.name: Roar
attacks.effect: Switch out your opponent's Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)

fullCardInfo.name: Boltund
fullCardInfo.description:
It sends electricity through its legs to boost their
strength. Running at top speed, it easily breaks
50 mph.

fullCardInfo.name: Natu
fullCardInfo.description:
Because its wings aren't yet fully grown, it has to
hop to get around. It is always staring
at something.

fullCardInfo.name: Xatu
fullCardInfo.description:
This odd Pokémon can see both the past and
the future. It eyes the sun's movement all day.

fullCardInfo.name: Misdreavus
fullCardInfo.description:
It loves to bite and yank people's hair from
behind without warning, just to see their
shocked reactions.

attacks.name: Double Spin
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.

fullCardInfo.name: Mismagius
fullCardInfo.description:
Its cries sound like incantations to torment the
foe. It appears where you least expect it.

fullCardInfo.name: Gothita
fullCardInfo.description:
Beware of touching the ribbon-shaped feelers
that control its psychic power. Gothita will begin
bawling if you do.

fullCardInfo.name: Gothorita
fullCardInfo.description:
Using its psychic power, it arranges pebbles to
form the shapes of constellations. Some people
believe this Pokémon came from outer space.

fullCardInfo.name: Spritzee
fullCardInfo.description:
The scent its body gives off enraptures those
who smell it. Noble ladies had no shortage of
love for Spritzee.

fullCardInfo.name: Aromatisse
fullCardInfo.description:
The scents Aromatisse can produce range from
sweet smells that bolster allies to foul smells
that sap an opponent's will to fight.

attacks.name: Confounding Cologne
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

fullCardInfo.name: Swirlix
fullCardInfo.description:
The sweet smell of cotton candy perfumes
Swirlix's fluffy fur. This Pokémon spits out
sticky string to tangle up its enemies.

attacks.name: Cotton Guard
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes −20 damage from attacks.

fullCardInfo.name: Slurpuff
fullCardInfo.description:
Slurpuff's fur contains a lot of air, making it soft
to the touch and lighter than it looks.

attacks.name: Draining Kiss
attacks.effect: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Carbink
fullCardInfo.description:
It's said that somewhere in the world, there's
a mineral vein housing a large pack of slumbering
Carbink. It's also said that this pack has a queen.

attacks.name: Guard Press
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes −30 damage from attacks.

fullCardInfo.name: Sandslash
fullCardInfo.description:
It climbs trees by hooking on with its sharp claws.
Sandslash shares the berries it gathers, dropping
them down to Sandshrew waiting below the tree.

fullCardInfo.name: Sudowoodo
fullCardInfo.description:
To avoid being attacked, it does nothing but
mimic a tree. It hates water and flees from rain.

attacks.name: Fight Back
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has damage on it, this attack does 50 more damage.

fullCardInfo.name: Hippowdon
fullCardInfo.description:
It brandishes its gaping mouth in a display of
fearsome strength. It raises vast quantities of
sand while attacking.

attacks.name: Crashing Fangs
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, during your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

fullCardInfo.name: Sandile
fullCardInfo.description:
They live hidden under hot desert sands in order
to keep their body temperature from dropping.

fullCardInfo.name: Archen
fullCardInfo.description:
Archen is said to be the ancestor of bird
Pokémon. It lived in treetops, eating berries
and bug Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Pancham
fullCardInfo.description:
Wanting to make sure it's taken seriously,
Pancham's always giving others a glare.
But if it's not focusing, it ends up smiling.

fullCardInfo.name: Bewear
fullCardInfo.description:
The moves it uses to take down its prey would
make a martial artist jealous. It tucks subdued
prey under its arms to carry them to its nest.

fullCardInfo.name: Sandygast
fullCardInfo.description:
It flings sand into the eyes of its prey and tries to
close in on them while they're blinded, but since
Sandygast is so slow, the prey tends to escape.

attacks.name: Sand Attack
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, if the Defending Pokémon tries to use an attack, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack doesn't happen.

fullCardInfo.name: Palossand
fullCardInfo.description:
From the hollows in its arms, it fires the bones
of its victims, which are all dried up after being
drained of their vitality.

attacks.name: Life Sucker
attacks.effect: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Carkol
fullCardInfo.description:
Due to the coal tar created inside it, the heap of
coal on Carkol's back never falls apart, even
when the Pokémon rolls around at high speeds.

fullCardInfo.name: Murkrow
fullCardInfo.description:
If spotted, it will lure an unwary person
into chasing it, then lose the pursuer on
mountain trails.

fullCardInfo.name: Skorupi
fullCardInfo.description:
It attacks using the claws on its tail. Once locked
in its grip, its prey is unable to move as this
Pokémon's poison seeps in.

fullCardInfo.name: Mareanie
fullCardInfo.description:
It has excellent regenerative capabilities. Legs
that a Mareanie has lost have a very bitter,
astringent flavor—they're not suitable as food.

attacks.name: Poison Sting
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

fullCardInfo.name: Toxapex
fullCardInfo.description:
Toxapex gets into fierce battles with Bruxish over
areas where warm ocean currents flow, but the
odds are always against it.

attacks.name: Venoshock
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is Poisoned, this attack does 70 more damage.

fullCardInfo.name: Impidimp
fullCardInfo.description:
They live in groups, pestering and playing pranks
on each other to polish their troublemaking skills.

fullCardInfo.name: Morgrem
fullCardInfo.description:
This Pokémon absorbs negative emotions and
turns them into energy. It's popular with people
who tend to think gloomy thoughts.

attacks.name: False Surrender
attacks.effect: This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Grimmsnarl
fullCardInfo.description:
It lives deep within the forest. Even after evolving
into this splendid form, it hasn't given up on its
petty misdeeds and pranks.

fullCardInfo.name: Ferroseed
fullCardInfo.description:
Mossy caves are their preferred dwellings.
Enzymes contained in mosses help Ferroseed's
spikes grow big and strong.

fullCardInfo.name: Ferrothorn
fullCardInfo.description:
Its spikes are harder than steel. This Pokémon
crawls across rock walls by stabbing the spikes
on its feelers into the stone.

attacks.name: Guard Press
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes −20 damage from attacks.

fullCardInfo.name: Durant
fullCardInfo.description:
They lay their eggs deep inside their nests.
When attacked by Heatmor, they retaliate
using their massive mandibles.

attacks.name: Bite Together
attacks.effect: If Durant is on your Bench, this attack does 40 more damage.

fullCardInfo.name: Pidgey
fullCardInfo.description:
It is docile and prefers to avoid conflict.
If disturbed, however, it can ferociously strike back.

fullCardInfo.name: Pidgeotto
fullCardInfo.description:
Very protective of its sprawling territorial area,
this Pokémon will fiercely peck at any intruder.

fullCardInfo.name: Ambipom
fullCardInfo.description:
It uses its tails for everything. If it wraps both
of its tails around you and gives you a squeeze,
that's proof it really likes you.

attacks.name: Excited Tail
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage for each heads. If this Pokémon has Lucky Mittens attached, flip 4 coins instead.

fullCardInfo.name: Miltank
fullCardInfo.description:
This Pokémon needs to be milked every day,
or else it will fall ill. The flavor of Miltank milk
changes with the seasons.

attacks.name: Body Slam
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

fullCardInfo.name: Zigzagoon
fullCardInfo.description:
A Pokémon with abundant curiosity. It shows an
interest in everything, so it always zigzags.

fullCardInfo.name: Linoone
fullCardInfo.description:
It uses its explosive speed and razor-sharp claws
to bring down prey. Running along winding
paths is not its strong suit.

fullCardInfo.name: Whismur
fullCardInfo.description:
When Whismur cries, the sound of its own voice
startles it, making the Pokémon cry even louder.
It cries until it's exhausted, then it falls asleep.

fullCardInfo.name: Delcatty
fullCardInfo.description:
It is highly popular among female Trainers for its
sublime fur. It does not keep a nest.

abilities.name: Search for Friends
abilities.effect: Once during your turn, when you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may put a Supporter card from your discard pile into your hand.

fullCardInfo.name: Swablu
fullCardInfo.description:
It can't relax if it or its surroundings are not clean.
It wipes off dirt with its wings.

attacks.name: Sing
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

fullCardInfo.name: Chatot
fullCardInfo.description:
It can learn and speak human words. If they gather,
they all learn the same saying.

attacks.name: Sleepy Song
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

fullCardInfo.name: Patrat
fullCardInfo.description:
Extremely cautious, one of them will always be on
the lookout, but it won't notice a foe coming
from behind.

fullCardInfo.name: Watchog
fullCardInfo.description:
When they see an enemy, their tails stand high,
and they spit the seeds of berries stored in their
cheek pouches.

attacks.name: Biting Fang
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 more damage.

fullCardInfo.name: Stoutland
fullCardInfo.description:
Stoutland is immensely proud of its impressive
moustache. It's said that moustache length is what
determines social standing among this species.

attacks.name: Dangerous Bite
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Basic Pokémon, this attack does 70 more damage.

fullCardInfo.name: Rookidee
fullCardInfo.description:
The females are fussier than the males. If another
creature dirties a female Rookidee's wings, it'll
peck the offender relentlessly in a burning rage.

fullCardInfo.name: Wooloo
fullCardInfo.description:
If its fleece grows too long, Wooloo won't be
able to move. Cloth made with the wool of this
Pokémon is surprisingly strong.

attacks.name: Continuous Tumble
attacks.effect: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 40 damage for each heads.

fullCardInfo.name: Prank Spinner
fullCardInfo.effect: A card from among both player's hands is chosen at random, revealed to the other player, and shuffled into its owner's deck.

fullCardInfo.name: Plume Fossil
fullCardInfo.effect:
Play this card as if it were a 40-HP Basic {C} Pokémon.
At any time during your turn, you may discard this card from play.
This card can't retreat.

fullCardInfo.name: Hitting Hammer
fullCardInfo.effect: Flip 2 coins. If both of them are heads, discard a random Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Cover Fossil
fullCardInfo.effect:
Play this card as if it were a 40-HP Basic {C} Pokémon.
At any time during your turn, you may discard this card from play.
This card can't retreat.

fullCardInfo.name: Flame Patch
fullCardInfo.effect: Attach a {R} Energy from your discard pile to your Active {R} Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Sitrus Berry
fullCardInfo.effect: At the end of each turn, if the Pokémon this card is attached to has half of its maximum HP or less remaining, heal 30 damage from it. If you do, discard this card.

fullCardInfo.name: Lucky Mittens
fullCardInfo.effect: Whenever your opponent's Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from an attack used by the Pokémon this card is attached to, draw a card.

fullCardInfo.name: Marlon
fullCardInfo.effect: Heal 70 damage from 1 of your Carracosta or Jellicent.

fullCardInfo.name: Hala
fullCardInfo.effect: During your opponent's next turn, if your Hariyama or Crabominable would be Knocked Out by damage from an attack, it is not Knocked Out and its remaining HP becomes 10.

fullCardInfo.name: May
fullCardInfo.effect: Put 2 random Pokémon from your deck into your hand. For each Pokémon you put into your hand in this way, choose a Pokémon to shuffle from your hand into your deck.

fullCardInfo.name: Fantina
fullCardInfo.effect: Take a {P} Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to each of your Drifblim and Mismagius.

fullCardInfo.name: Copycat
fullCardInfo.effect: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Draw a card for each card in your opponent's hand.

fullCardInfo.name: Rillaboom
fullCardInfo.description:
The one with the best drumming techniques
becomes the boss of the troop. It has a gentle
disposition and values harmony among its group.

abilities.name: Captivating Rhythm
abilities.effect: Once during your turn, you may flip a coin. If heads, switch in 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon to the Active Spot.

fullCardInfo.name: Chandelure
fullCardInfo.description:
In homes illuminated by Chandelure instead of
lights, funerals were a constant occurrence—
or so it's said.

abilities.name: Slow Sear
abilities.effect: Once during your turn, you may discard the top card of your opponent's deck.

fullCardInfo.name: Jellicent
fullCardInfo.description:
Whenever a full moon hangs in the night sky,
schools of Jellicent gather near the surface of
the sea, waiting for their prey to appear.

abilities.name: Bouncy Body
abilities.effect: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, take a {W} Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Cofagrigus
fullCardInfo.description:
There are many depictions of Cofagrigus
decorating ancient tombs. They're symbols
of the wealth that kings of bygone eras had.

attacks.name: Soul Shot
attacks.effect: Discard 2 cards from your hand. If you can't discard 2 cards, this attack does nothing.

fullCardInfo.name: Gothita
fullCardInfo.description:
Beware of touching the ribbon-shaped feelers
that control its psychic power. Gothita will begin
bawling if you do.

fullCardInfo.name: Hippowdon
fullCardInfo.description:
It brandishes its gaping mouth in a display of
fearsome strength. It raises vast quantities of
sand while attacking.

attacks.name: Crashing Fangs
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, during your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

fullCardInfo.name: Hydreigon
fullCardInfo.description:
It's said that Hydreigon grew ferocious because
people in times long past loathed it, considering
it to be evil incarnate and attacking it relentlessly.

abilities.name: Roar in Unison
abilities.effect: Once during your turn, you may take 2 {D} Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to this Pokémon. If you do, do 30 damage to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Hyper Ray
attacks.effect: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Corviknight
fullCardInfo.description:
Although its wings have partly turned to steel and
become heavy as a result, this Pokémon flies
through the skies with ease.

attacks.name: Iron Wings
attacks.effect: Discard 2 {M} Energy from this Pokémon. During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes −50 damage from attacks.

fullCardInfo.name: Oricorio
fullCardInfo.description:
This form of Oricorio has sipped yellow nectar.
It uses nimble steps to approach opponents,
then knocks them out with electric punches.

abilities.name: Safeguard
abilities.effect: Prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from your opponent's Pokémon ex.

fullCardInfo.name: Zeraora
fullCardInfo.description:
It electrifies its claws and tears its opponents
apart with them. Even if they dodge its attack,
they'll be electrocuted by the flying sparks.

abilities.name: Thunderclap Flash
abilities.effect: At the end of your first turn, take a {L} Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to this Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Exeggcute
fullCardInfo.description: Though it may look like it's just a bunch of eggs, it's a proper Pokémon. Exeggcute communicates with others of its kind via telepathy, apparently.

attacks.name: Growth Spurt
attacks.effect: Take a {G} Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to this Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Exeggutor
fullCardInfo.description: Each of Exeggutor's three heads is thinking different thoughts. The three don't seem to be very interested in one another.

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Powerful Bloom
attacks.effect: Flip a coin for each Energy attached to this Pokémon. This attack does 50 damage for each heads.

fullCardInfo.name: Snivy
fullCardInfo.description: Being exposed to sunlight makes its movements swifter. It uses vines more adeptly than its hands.

fullCardInfo.name: Servine
fullCardInfo.description: It moves along the ground as if sliding. Its swift movements befuddle its foes, and it then attacks with a vine whip.

fullCardInfo.name: Serperior
fullCardInfo.description: It only gives its all against strong opponents who are not fazed by the glare from Serperior's noble eyes.

abilities.name: Jungle Totem
abilities.effect: Each {G} Energy attached to your {G} Pokémon provides 2 {G} Energy. This effect doesn't stack.

fullCardInfo.name: Morelull
fullCardInfo.description: Pokémon living in the forest eat the delicious caps on Morelull's head. The caps regrow overnight.

fullCardInfo.name: Shiinotic
fullCardInfo.description: Its flickering spores lure in prey and put them to sleep. Once this Pokémon has its prey snoozing, it drains their vitality with its fingertips.

attacks.name: Flickering Spores
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

fullCardInfo.name: Dhelmise
fullCardInfo.description: After a piece of seaweed merged with debris from a sunken ship, it was reborn as this ghost Pokémon.

attacks.name: Energy Whip
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has at least 3 extra {G} Energy attached, this attack does 70 more damage.

fullCardInfo.name: Ponyta
fullCardInfo.description: It can't run properly when it's newly born. As it races around with others of its kind, its legs grow stronger.

attacks.name: Stomp
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

fullCardInfo.name: Rapidash
fullCardInfo.description: This Pokémon can be seen galloping through fields at speeds of up to 150 mph, its fiery mane fluttering in the wind.

attacks.name: Rising Lunge
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 60 more damage.

fullCardInfo.name: Magmar
fullCardInfo.description: Magmar dispatches its prey with fire. But it regrets this habit once it realizes that it has burned its intended prey to a charred crisp.

attacks.name: Fire Blast
attacks.effect: Discard 2 {R} Energy from this Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Larvesta
fullCardInfo.description: This Pokémon was called the Larva That Stole the Sun. The fire Larvesta spouts from its horns can cut right through a sheet of iron.

fullCardInfo.name: Volcarona
fullCardInfo.description: Its burning body causes it to be unpopular in hot parts of the world, but in cold ones, Volcarona is revered as an embodiment of the sun.

attacks.name: Volcanic Ash
attacks.effect: Discard 2 {R} Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 80 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Salandit
fullCardInfo.description: It taunts its prey and lures them into narrow, rocky areas where it then sprays them with toxic gas to make them dizzy and take them down.

attacks.name: Venoshock
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is Poisoned, this attack does 40 more damage.

fullCardInfo.name: Salazzle
fullCardInfo.description: Salazzle makes its opponents light-headed with poisonous gas, then captivates them with alluring movements to turn them into loyal servants.

attacks.name: Poison Claws
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

fullCardInfo.name: Magikarp
fullCardInfo.description: An underpowered, pathetic Pokémon. It may jump high on rare occasions but never more than seven feet.

attacks.name: Leap Out
attacks.effect: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Rampaging Whirlpool
attacks.effect: Discard a random Energy from among the Energy attached to all Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's).

fullCardInfo.name: Vaporeon
fullCardInfo.description: It lives close to water. Its long tail is ridged with a fin, which is often mistaken for a mermaid's.

abilities.name: Wash Out
abilities.effect: As often as you like during your turn, you may move a {W} Energy from 1 of your Benched {W} Pokémon to your Active {W} Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Finneon
fullCardInfo.description: The line running down its side can store sunlight. It shines vividly at night.

fullCardInfo.name: Lumineon
fullCardInfo.description: With its shining light, it lures its prey close. However, the light also happens to attract ferocious fish Pokémon—its natural predators.

attacks.name: Aqua Liner
attacks.effect: This attack does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Chewtle
fullCardInfo.description: Its large front tooth is still growing in. When the tooth itches, this Pokémon will bite another Chewtle's horn, and the two Pokémon will tussle.

fullCardInfo.name: Drednaw
fullCardInfo.description: Its massive, jagged teeth can crush a boulder in a single bite. This Pokémon has an extremely vicious disposition.

attacks.name: Crunch
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, discard a random Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Cramorant
fullCardInfo.description: It's so strong that it can knock out some opponents in a single hit, but it also may forget what it's battling midfight.

attacks.name: Dive
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from—and effects of—attacks done to this Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Pikachu
fullCardInfo.description: When it is angered, it immediately discharges the energy stored in the pouches in its cheeks.

attacks.name: Circle Circuit
attacks.effect: This attack does 10 damage for each of your Benched {L} Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Raichu
fullCardInfo.description: Its tail discharges electricity into the ground, protecting it from getting shocked.

attacks.name: Gigashock
attacks.effect: This attack also does 20 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Electabuzz
fullCardInfo.description: Many power plants keep Ground-type Pokémon around as a defense against Electabuzz that come seeking electricity.

attacks.name: Thunder Spear
attacks.effect: This attack does 40 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Joltik
fullCardInfo.description: Joltik can be found clinging to other Pokémon. It's soaking up static electricity because it can't produce a charge on its own.

fullCardInfo.name: Galvantula
fullCardInfo.description: It launches electrified fur from its abdomen as its means of attack. Opponents hit by the fur could be in for three full days and nights of paralysis.

attacks.name: Electroweb
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

fullCardInfo.name: Dedenne
fullCardInfo.description: It's small and its electricity-generating organ is not fully developed, so it uses its tail to absorb electricity from people's homes and charge itself.

attacks.name: Thunder Shock
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Genome Hacking
attacks.effect: Choose 1 of your opponent's Active Pokémon's attacks and use it as this attack.

fullCardInfo.name: Sigilyph
fullCardInfo.description: Psychic power allows these Pokémon to fly. Some say they were the guardians of an ancient city. Others say they were the guardians' emissaries.

attacks.name: Spike Draw
attacks.effect: Draw a card.

fullCardInfo.name: Elgyem
fullCardInfo.description: If this Pokémon stands near a TV, strange scenery will appear on the screen. That scenery is said to be from its home.

fullCardInfo.name: Beheeyem
fullCardInfo.description: Whenever a Beheeyem visits a farm, a Dubwool mysteriously disappears.

attacks.name: Mind Jack
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 more damage for each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Flabébé
fullCardInfo.description: This Pokémon can draw forth the power hidden within blooming wild flowers. It is particularly fond of red flowers.

attacks.name: Hypnotic Gaze
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

fullCardInfo.name: Floette
fullCardInfo.description: This Pokémon draws forth what power is left in withered flowers to make them healthy again. It holds a red flower.

attacks.name: Leaf Drain
attacks.effect: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Florges
fullCardInfo.description: This Pokémon creates an impressive flower garden in its territory. It draws forth the power of the red flowers around its neck.

attacks.name: Bloomshine
attacks.effect: Heal 20 damage from each of your Pokémon.

fullCardInfo.name: Swirlix
fullCardInfo.description: It eats its own weight in sugar every day. If it doesn't get enough sugar, it becomes incredibly grumpy.

fullCardInfo.name: Slurpuff
fullCardInfo.description: By taking in a person's scent, it can sniff out their mental and physical condition. It's hoped that this skill will have many medical applications.

fullCardInfo.name: Mankey
fullCardInfo.description: It lives in groups in the treetops. If it loses sight of its group, it becomes infuriated by its loneliness.

attacks.name: Focus Fist
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

fullCardInfo.name: Primeape
fullCardInfo.description: It becomes wildly furious if it even senses someone looking at it. It chases anyone that meets its glare.

fullCardInfo.name: Geodude
fullCardInfo.description: Geodude that have lived a long life have had all their edges smoothed out until they're totally round. They also have a calm, quiet disposition.

fullCardInfo.name: Graveler
fullCardInfo.description: It climbs up cliffs as it heads toward the peak of a mountain. As soon as it reaches the summit, it rolls back down the way it came.

fullCardInfo.name: Golem
fullCardInfo.description: When Golem grow old, they stop shedding their shells. Those that have lived a long, long time have shells green with moss.

attacks.name: Guard Press
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes −30 damage from attacks.

fullCardInfo.name: Marshadow
fullCardInfo.description: It slips into the shadows of others and mimics their powers and movements. As it improves, it becomes stronger than those it's imitating.

attacks.name: Revenge
attacks.effect: If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an attack during your opponent's last turn, this attack does 60 more damage.

fullCardInfo.name: Stonjourner
fullCardInfo.description: The elemental composition of the rocks that form its body were found to match the bedrock of a land far away from this Pokémon's habitat.

fullCardInfo.name: Koffing
fullCardInfo.description: Its body is full of poisonous gas. It floats into garbage dumps, seeking out the fumes of raw, rotting trash.

attacks.name: Division
attacks.effect: Put 1 random Koffing from your deck onto your Bench.

fullCardInfo.name: Weezing
fullCardInfo.description: If one of the twin Koffing inflates, the other one deflates. It constantly mixes its poisonous gases.

attacks.name: Smokescreen
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, if the Defending Pokémon tries to use an attack, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack doesn't happen.

fullCardInfo.name: Purrloin
